The defects are calque/naturalness only: "In words" is rendered literally as "Dengan kata-kata" three times where a Malay technical writer would write "Dengan kata lain"; "mathematical machinery" becomes "jentera matematik" (industrial machinery); "three or fewer" becomes "tiga atau lebih sedikit" instead of "tiga atau kurang", which reads momentarily as "three or a bit more" before the next clause ("sekurang-kurangnya empat diperlukan") disambiguates it; "chosen uniformly at random" has inverted adverb order as "dipilih secara seragam rawak". None of these mislead a learner. Separately, non-prose: the blank line before "## Perwakilan Kraus" (line 376/377) is missing relative to EN.

examples.source: In words, the way the implementation works is as follows.
examples.translation: Dengan kata-kata, cara pelaksanaan ini berfungsi adalah seperti berikut.
examples.suggested: Dengan kata lain, cara pelaksanaan ini berfungsi adalah seperti berikut.
examples.why: "Dengan kata-kata" is a literal calque of "in words" and is not idiomatic Malay; the fixed expression is "Dengan kata lain" (or "Secara ringkasnya"). Repeated four times across the file, so it reads as MT throughout those transitions.

examples.source: It is not possible to find a Kraus representation for the completely depolarizing channel Omega having three or fewer Kraus matrices; at least four are required for this channel.
examples.translation: Tidak mungkin untuk mencari perwakilan Kraus bagi saluran depolarizing sepenuhnya $\Omega$ yang mempunyai tiga atau lebih sedikit matriks Kraus; sekurang-kurangnya empat diperlukan untuk saluran ini.
examples.suggested: ...yang mempunyai tiga atau kurang matriks Kraus; sekurang-kurangnya empat diperlukan untuk saluran ini.
examples.why: "tiga atau lebih sedikit" is a word-for-word rendering of "three or fewer" and parses on first read as "three or slightly more" — the opposite bound. The following clause repairs the meaning, so this is a naturalness slip rather than drift, but a native writer would use "tiga atau kurang".

examples.source: To answer this question, we'll require some additional mathematical machinery.
examples.translation: Untuk menjawab soalan ini, kita memerlukan beberapa jentera matematik tambahan.
examples.suggested: Untuk menjawab soalan ini, kita memerlukan beberapa peralatan matematik tambahan.
examples.why: "jentera" means industrial machinery/engines; the figurative English sense of "machinery" (apparatus, toolkit) does not carry over. "peralatan" or "perkakas matematik" is the conventional rendering.

examples.source: ...by applying to it one of the four Pauli matrices (including the identity matrix) chosen uniformly at random.
examples.translation: ...dengan menerapkan padanya salah satu daripada empat matriks Pauli (termasuk matriks identiti) yang dipilih secara seragam rawak.
examples.suggested: ...yang dipilih secara rawak seragam.
examples.why: Adverb order is calqued from English "uniformly at random"; Malay places the head first — "secara rawak seragam". Meaning is recoverable, so this is a stylistic slip only.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — every plan's numbers and conditions survive intact (400-minute Flex minimum, 10,000-minute Premium threshold, the 180-minute/12-month promo, "jika anda memperuntukkan semua minit... anda tidak boleh mencipta instance baharu" preserves the conditional correctly), register is consistently standard "anda", and "instance"/"QPU"/"pelan" are kept in English consistently. Two isolated word-choice calques hold it back from PASS: "28-day rolling window" → "tempoh gelinding 28 hari" ("gelinding" is physical tumbling/rolling of an object, never used for a rolling time window — "tempoh bergulir 28 hari" or keeping "rolling" is the convention), and "entirely dedicated on-premises quantum system" → "sepenuhnya berdedikasi", where "berdedikasi" in Malay means devoted/committed of a *person*, not resource-dedicated ("khusus" is the correct term). Neither misleads a learner; both read as MT word-for-word substitution in an otherwise natural page.

examples.source: up to 10 minutes per 28-day rolling window
examples.translation: sehingga 10 minit dalam tempoh gelinding 28 hari
examples.suggested: sehingga 10 minit bagi setiap tempoh bergulir 28 hari
examples.why: "gelinding" denotes an object physically rolling/tumbling; no Malay technical writer would use it for a rolling time window. Meaning is still recoverable, so it is a naturalness slip rather than drift.

examples.source: Access an entirely dedicated on-premises quantum system that is serviced and maintained by IBM Quantum.
examples.translation: Akses sistem kuantum dalam premis yang sepenuhnya berdedikasi, yang diselenggarakan oleh IBM Quantum.
examples.suggested: Akses sistem kuantum dalam premis yang sepenuhnya khusus (dedicated), yang diservis dan diselenggarakan oleh IBM Quantum.
examples.why: "berdedikasi" is a false friend — in Malay it describes a devoted person, not an exclusively-allocated resource; "khusus" is the conventional rendering. Also "serviced and maintained" is collapsed into the single verb "diselenggarakan", a small flattening that does not change the offering.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — the tricky pedagogical passages survive intact (the gradient/error-mitigation nuance in "Apabila kamu jauh dari parameter optimum, kecerunan yang curam mungkin kelihatan… kecerunan menjadi lebih kecil, dan lebih mudah dihapuskan oleh ralat", and the Step 4 reading of fun/nfev/success=False/x with "lapan sudut putaran"), and domain terms are correct and consistent (fungsi kos, nilai jangkaan, pengoptimum klasik, penumpuan, parameter variasi, pengurus lulus, hingar). The one real defect is structural: the third bullet of the session-options list is emitted TWICE — line 266 is the raw untranslated English "Estimator's built-in resilience: As with optimization, Estimator has built-in settings…" and line 267 is its Malay translation, so the reader sees a duplicated half-English list item the source does not have (the duplicate's link also points at /guides/error-mitigation-and-suppression-techniques while the Malay one points at /guides/configure-error-mitigation). Secondary: the file uses casual "kamu" consistently rather than the standard "anda" convention (MINOR, not misleading), and a couple of stiff renderings — "Iterasi seterusnya pengoptimum klasik tidak akan bergilir" for "will not be queued" (a native writer would say "tidak akan beratur dalam giliran") and "qubit yang melahu" for "idling qubits". None of these mislead a learner.

examples.source: * Estimator's built-in resilience: As with optimization, Estimator has built-in settings for resilience against errors… refer to the [documentation](/guides/error-mitigation-and-suppression-techniques).
examples.translation:
* Estimator's built-in resilience: As with optimization, Estimator has built-in settings… [documentation](/guides/error-mitigation-and-suppression-techniques).
* Daya tahan terbina dalam Estimator: Seperti pengoptimuman, Estimator mempunyai tetapan terbina dalam untuk daya tahan terhadap ralat… rujuk [dokumentasi](/guides/configure-error-mitigation).
examples.suggested: Delete the leftover English bullet at line 266; keep only the Malay bullet at 267 (and reconcile the link target with the EN source).
examples.why: The source has ONE bullet; the translation renders it as two consecutive bullets — one still fully in English — so the list reads as a duplicated, half-untranslated item. The two copies also point at different guide URLs, which a reader will take as two different references.

examples.source: If you have questions about that… / the least busy backend available to you / How much error mitigation do you want to use? / Congratulations! You have run a VQE calculation
examples.translation: Jika kamu mempunyai soalan tentang perkara itu… / Backend yang paling kurang sibuk yang tersedia untuk kamu / Berapa banyak pengurangan ralat yang kamu mahu gunakan? / Tahniah! Kamu telah menjalankan pengiraan VQE
examples.suggested: anda (throughout)
examples.why: The file uses casual 'kamu' consistently where the house convention is standard 'anda'. Consistent and non-misleading, so MINOR — but it reads more familiar than the English's neutral instructional register.

examples.source: Subsequent iterations of the classical optimizer will not be queued.
examples.translation: Iterasi seterusnya pengoptimum klasik tidak akan bergilir.
examples.suggested: Iterasi seterusnya oleh pengoptimum klasik tidak akan perlu beratur dalam giliran.
examples.why: 'tidak akan bergilir' is a calque of 'will not be queued' that in Malay reads as 'will not take turns' rather than 'will not have to wait in the queue'; the surrounding paragraph already uses 'masa giliran', so the natural phrasing is available. Meaning is recoverable from context, hence naturalness rather than drift.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout with consistent "anda" register and stable job/workload terminology (job→"kerja", workload→"beban kerja", backend/optimization_level kept as code); the status list (QUEUED/INITIALIZING/RUNNING/DONE) preserves every caveat, including "Keutamaan barisan pada masa ini berdasarkan sejauh mana anda telah menggunakan Qiskit Serverless", and the source's own 30-vs-10 circuit mismatch is carried over faithfully rather than silently "corrected".

Meaning is faithful throughout — no drift, no hallucination, no dropped caveats; the tricky exclusive=true/false bullets, the multi-tenancy list, and the "no shared cluster-wide quantum access" caveat all survive intact, and the informal "ty" register holds consistently ("Pamiętaj, że...", "Zapoznaj się z...", "Przykładowe pliki znajdziesz w..."). Two naturalness slips only: "tryb ekskluzywny / zadanie nieekskluzywne" leans on a false friend — in everyday Polish "ekskluzywny" means upscale/posh, and a Polish HPC writer would say "tryb wyłączny" / "na wyłączność"; and English "capacity" is flattened to "wydajność" (performance) twice ("według kryteriów wydajności i współczynnika błędów", "Wydajność i priorytet użycia QPU"), where "przepustowość"/"pojemność" is the intended sense. Neither misleads a learner about how the plugin behaves.

examples.source: An exclusive mode job gets all execution lanes and cannot run at the same time as a non-exclusive job
examples.translation: Zadanie w trybie ekskluzywnym uzyskuje wszystkie pasma wykonania i nie może działać jednocześnie z zadaniem nieekskluzywnym
examples.suggested: Zadanie w trybie wyłącznym uzyskuje wszystkie pasma wykonania i nie może działać jednocześnie z zadaniem niewyłącznym
examples.why: "Ekskluzywny" in Polish colloquially means upscale/exclusive-as-in-luxury; the scheduling sense is "wyłączny" / "na wyłączność". Grammatical and understandable, but reads as a calque rather than native HPC prose.

examples.source: by capacity and error rate qualifiers … The capacity and priority of the QPU usage is solely managed through Slurm
examples.translation: według kryteriów wydajności i współczynnika błędów … Wydajność i priorytet użycia QPU są zarządzane wyłącznie przez Slurm
examples.suggested: według kryteriów przepustowości i współczynnika błędów … Przepustowość i priorytet użycia QPU…
examples.why: "Capacity" here is available throughput/allocation to be scheduled, not "performance" (wydajność). Consistent within the file, so not misleading, but it slightly shifts what Slurm is said to manage.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — I checked the load-bearing claims paragraph-by-paragraph and found no drift: the Eastin–Knill statement ("o odległości co najmniej 2 … jest dyskretny, a zatem nie jest uniwersalny"), the magic-state measurement branches (0 → no S gate → T|ψ⟩; 1 → S gate → ST†|ψ⟩ = T|ψ⟩), the distillation gating ("Wszystkie bloki wyjściowe poza jednym są mierzone"; any outcome 1 → restart), the CSS-code caveat ("działa tylko dla CSS codes — lecz działa w istocie dla *wszystkich* CSS codes"), and the transversal-gadget key point ("nigdy nie może spowodować propagacji błędów *wewnątrz pojedynczego bloku kodu*") all match the English exactly, including the deliberately odd Shor-code X↔Z assignment. The defects are grammatical/naturalness, not semantic: the term "bramka $T$" is frozen in the nominative in roughly eight places where Polish requires an oblique case — "zaimplementowanie bramka $T$", "zaaplikować bramka $T$", "skupmy się na bramka $T$", "w oryginalnym układzie bramka $T$" — which no native technical writer would produce and reads as raw MT at those spots. Second, the file splits its core terms: "qubit/qubitów" dominates but the "Error propagation for transversal gadgets" section switches wholesale to "kubitów/jednokubitowej", and "Stabilizer code" is rendered "kod Stabilizer" everywhere except the final line ("kody stabilizatora"). Third, "distiller" → "destylarnia" (a distillery/plant) is off for a circuit; "destylator" is the natural choice. None of this misleads a learner, but the case-frozen "bramka $T$" is worth a targeted fix.

examples.source: it appears that we still need to apply a $T$ gate to a $\vert{+}\rangle$ state … let's focus in on $T$ gates specifically
examples.translation: wydaje się, że i tak musimy zaaplikować bramka $T$ do stanu $\vert{+}\rangle$ … skupmy się na bramka $T$
examples.suggested: musimy zaaplikować bramkę $T$ … skupmy się na bramce $T$
examples.why: "bramka" is frozen in the nominative where Polish grammar requires accusative (bramkę) / locative (bramce); recurs ~8 times and reads as untouched machine output.

examples.source: For a transversal implementation of a single-qubit gate … no multiple-qubit gates are involved.
examples.translation: W przypadku transversal implementacji bramki jednokubitowej … nie są zaangażowane bramki wielokubitowe.
examples.suggested: …implementacji gate'u jedno-qubitowego … gate'y wieloqubitowe
examples.why: The file uses "qubit/gate" spellings everywhere else ("gate'y dwu-qubitowe", "qubicie kontrolnym"); this one section flips to "kubit/bramka", a within-file split of two core terms.

examples.source: a special type of circuit known as a *distiller*
examples.translation: specjalnego układu zwanego *destylarnią*
examples.suggested: układu zwanego *destylatorem*
examples.why: "Destylarnia" denotes a distillery/production plant, not a device or circuit; "destylator" is the natural Polish rendering for a distilling apparatus.

examples.source: extends this method to arbitrary stabilizer codes
examples.translation: rozszerza tę metodę na dowolne kody stabilizatora
examples.suggested: rozszerza tę metodę na dowolne kody Stabilizer
examples.why: Last line abandons the "kod Stabilizer" convention held consistently throughout the rest of the file ("Stabilizer code", "kodu Stabilizer", "generatorów Stabilizer").

editor_note: Two false-friend mistranslations of domain vocabulary. In the QUBO objective list, "an additional term to minimize $P$" becomes "dodatkowy termin do minimalizacji $P$" — Polish "termin" means deadline/appointment; the mathematical term is "człon" (or "wyraz"), so the bullet reads as nonsense to a Polish reader. In Step 2, "the primitive settings, such as estimator_shots ... define the quantum Estimator and Sampler configurations" becomes "ustawienia prymitywne", i.e. "primitive/crude settings" — an adjective — rather than "ustawienia prymitywów" (settings *of the primitives*), which actively misleads about Qiskit's Estimator/Sampler primitives. Also, VQE is over-translated to "wariacyjnym rozwiązywaczu własnym (VQE)", dropping "kwantowy" and coining a non-conventional "rozwiązywacz własny"; and "circuit" is split within the file between "układ" (line ~217 "Całkowita liczba układów") and "obwód" (line ~219 "optymalizacji obwodów"). Separately, the frontmatter title diverges from the source: EN "Dynamic portfolio optimization with Global Data Quantum's Portfolio Optimizer" vs PL "Quantum Portfolio Optimizer - Funkcja Qiskit od Global Data Quantum", which drops the "dynamic portfolio optimization" topic entirely. The body prose is otherwise faithful, informal ("wybierz funkcję", "Poświęć chwilę"), and reads naturally.

examples.source: Comply with the investment restrictions, formulated in an additional term to minimize $P$.
examples.translation: Spełnienie ograniczeń inwestycyjnych, sformułowanych jako dodatkowy termin do minimalizacji $P$.
examples.suggested: Spełnienie ograniczeń inwestycyjnych, sformułowanych jako dodatkowy człon minimalizowany $P$.
examples.why: 'termin' is a false friend — in Polish it means deadline/appointment, never a mathematical term. The correct rendering of a term in an equation is 'człon' (or 'wyraz'). As written the bullet is meaningless.

examples.source: Additionally, the primitive settings, such as `estimator_shots`, `estimator_precision`, and `sampler_shots`, define the quantum Estimator and Sampler configurations for the optimization process.
examples.translation: Ponadto ustawienia prymitywne, takie jak `estimator_shots`, `estimator_precision` i `sampler_shots`, definiują konfiguracje Estimatora kwantowego i Samplera dla procesu optymalizacji.
examples.suggested: Ponadto ustawienia prymitywów, takie jak `estimator_shots`, `estimator_precision` i `sampler_shots`, definiują konfiguracje kwantowego Estimatora i Samplera dla procesu optymalizacji.
examples.why: 'primitive' here is the Qiskit noun (Estimator/Sampler primitives), not an adjective. 'ustawienia prymitywne' reads as 'crude/basic settings' and hides the primitives concept from the learner.

examples.source: We solve using a hybrid quantum-classical method based on the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE).
examples.translation: Rozwiązujemy problem za pomocą hybrydowej metody kwantowo-klasycznej opartej na wariacyjnym rozwiązywaczu własnym (VQE).
examples.suggested: ... opartej na wariacyjnym kwantowym solwerze stanów własnych (Variational Quantum Eigensolver, VQE).
examples.why: Non-conventional coinage 'rozwiązywacz własny' plus silent loss of 'Quantum'. Polish quantum writing keeps VQE / uses 'solwer stanów własnych'; note the same file already uses 'solwera' at line ~68, so the choice is also inconsistent.

examples.source: The total number of circuits depends on the `optimizer_settings` parameters ... The `ansatz_settings` dictionary configures the quantum circuit ansatz ... to improve circuit optimization performance
examples.translation: Całkowita liczba układów zależy od parametrów `optimizer_settings` ... Słownik `ansatz_settings` konfiguruje ansatz obwodu kwantowego ... w celu poprawy wydajności i efektywności optymalizacji obwodów
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering of 'circuit' (e.g. 'obwód kwantowy') and use it throughout: 'Całkowita liczba obwodów zależy od ...'.
examples.why: Within-file split of the core term 'circuit' between 'układ' and 'obwód' in adjacent paragraphs; a learner cannot tell whether 'liczba układów' counts the same objects as 'optymalizacja obwodów'.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout and idiomatic Polish with consistent informal "ty" ("Pomiń ten krok, jeśli masz już konto..."); the two load-bearing caveats survive intact — the note "Opłata zostanie naliczona dopiero po utworzeniu instancji planu Pay-As-You-Go i wysłaniu za jej pomocą obciążeń roboczych do QPU" correctly preserves the "not charged unless" condition without inverting it, and the <ins> cost warning plus the Open/free-plan escape clause are rendered accurately; plan names (Open, Pay-As-You-Go, Trial, Lite) and QPU are consistently kept in English as the field does.

Two genuine meaning defects. (1) The classical-control explanation is INVERTED: EN "the gates are not applied or applied depending on whether these classical control bits are 0 or 1, respectively" became "bramki są stosowane lub nie stosowane w zależności od tego, czy te klasyczne bity sterujące wynoszą 0 czy 1" — the PL order maps applied→0 and not-applied→1, the exact opposite of the source, and "respectively" is dropped, so a learner reads the X/Z corrections as firing on 0. (2) The file's final sentence is missing entirely: EN closes with the quantum-gate-teleportation example ("quantum gates can be implemented through a closely related process known as *quantum gate teleportation*, which uses teleportation to apply *operations* to qubits rather than communicating them"); the PL stops at "a jej różne warianty pojawiają się wciąż na nowo." — the whole illustrating example that motivates the claim is gone. Additionally, terminology is split within the file: "qubit" (most of the text) vs "kubit" (skrajnie lewym kubitem, kubitu Q, na kubicie B, kubity A i Q), and the character name is split "Alice" vs "Alicja"/"Alicji"; step 1 also calls Q/A "bit kontrolny"/"bit docelowy" when they are control/target *qubits*.

examples.source: the gates are not applied or applied depending on whether these classical control bits are 0 or 1, respectively
examples.translation: bramki są stosowane lub nie stosowane w zależności od tego, czy te klasyczne bity sterujące wynoszą $0$ czy $1.$
examples.suggested: bramki nie są stosowane albo są stosowane w zależności od tego, czy te klasyczne bity sterujące wynoszą odpowiednio $0$ czy $1.$
examples.why: Condition is inverted: PL pairs 'applied' with 0 and 'not applied' with 1, and drops 'respectively'. Directly contradicts step 3 (X applied when a=1).

examples.source: It really is a cornerstone of quantum information theory, and variations of it arise. For example, quantum gates can be implemented through a closely related process known as *quantum gate teleportation*, which uses teleportation to apply *operations* to qubits rather than communicating them.
examples.translation: Jest to prawdziwy kamień węgielny teorii informacji kwantowej, a jej różne warianty pojawiają się wciąż na nowo.
examples.suggested: …a jej warianty pojawiają się w wielu miejscach. Na przykład bramki kwantowe można zaimplementować poprzez blisko spokrewniony proces zwany *teleportacją bramek kwantowych*, który wykorzystuje teleportację do stosowania *operacji* na qubitach, zamiast do ich przesyłania.
examples.why: The entire closing sentence — the one concrete example backing the 'cornerstone' claim — is absent from the translation; the page ends mid-argument.

examples.source: qubit
examples.translation: kubit (skrajnie lewym kubitem / kubitu $\mathsf{B}$ / kubicie $\mathsf{B}$ / kubitu $\mathsf{Q}$ / kubity $\mathsf{A}$ i $\mathsf{Q}$)
examples.suggested: qubit — matching the dominant spelling used throughout the rest of the file
examples.why: Within-file split between 'qubit' (majority) and 'kubit'; the same paragraph (line 212-213) uses both spellings one line apart.

examples.source: with $\mathsf{Q}$ being the control and $\mathsf{A}$ being the target
examples.translation: gdzie $\mathsf{Q}$ jest bitem kontrolnym, a $\mathsf{A}$ jest bitem docelowym
examples.suggested: gdzie $\mathsf{Q}$ jest qubitem sterującym, a $\mathsf{A}$ qubitem docelowym
examples.why: Q and A are qubits, not bits; calling them 'bity' in a lesson that carefully separates classical bits from qubits blurs the very distinction being taught.

examples.source: which we already know is impossible by the no-cloning theorem
examples.translation: co wiemy już jest niemożliwe na mocy twierdzenia o zakazie klonowania
examples.suggested: co, jak już wiemy, jest niemożliwe na mocy twierdzenia o zakazie klonowania
examples.why: Calqued English relative clause without the Polish parenthetical construction; ungrammatical as written, and it recurs identically twice.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — the QEC narrative, the five-qubit split (three encoding / two "kubity pomocnicze do pomiaru stabilizatora"), the X-error mapping, and the closing claim "kod odwrócenia bitu wykrył i skorygował wiele błędów, co skutkuje mniejszą liczbą błędów ogółem" all match the English, and the informal "ty" register holds ("musisz być w stanie", "znajdziesz"). Three non-misleading slips: (1) the mathematical term "support" in "a discrete error set with support from the Pauli group" is rendered as the everyday "z wsparciem grupy Pauliego" ("with the help of"), where the intended sense is support/nośnik within the Pauli group; (2) the ISA acronym expansion ("instruction set architecture") is silently dropped, leaving "zwane obwodami i obserwablami ISA" unglossed for a first-time reader; (3) "Kody repetycyjne" is a calque — Polish literature conventionally uses "kody powtórzeniowe" — though it is understandable and used consistently. None of these invert a claim or mislead about the physics.

examples.source: Stabilizer codes critically focus on correcting a discrete error set with support from the Pauli group $\Pi^n$.
examples.translation: Kody stabilizatora skupiają się przede wszystkim na korygowaniu dyskretnego zbioru błędów z wsparciem grupy Pauliego $\Pi^n$.
examples.suggested: ...dyskretnego zbioru błędów o nośniku w grupie Pauliego $\Pi^n$.
examples.why: "Support" here is the mathematical support (the set the errors are drawn from), not assistance; "z wsparciem" reads as "with the help of the Pauli group", flattening a technical statement into a vague one.

examples.source: circuits and observables that conforms to the instructions and connectivity supported by the target system (referred to as instruction set architecture (ISA) circuits and observables)
examples.translation: obwody i obserwable zgodne z instrukcjami i połączeniami obsługiwanymi przez system docelowy (zwane obwodami i obserwablami ISA)
examples.suggested: ... (zwane obwodami i obserwablami ISA, od instruction set architecture — architektura zbioru instrukcji)
examples.why: The parenthetical exists precisely to introduce the acronym; dropping the expansion leaves "ISA" undefined on first use, so the tutorial teaches slightly less than the English.

examples.source: Repetition codes
examples.translation: Kody repetycyjne
examples.suggested: Kody powtórzeniowe
examples.why: Polish coding-theory literature standardly says "kod powtórzeniowy"; "repetycyjny" is a transparent calque. Used consistently, so not misleading — a conventionality nitpick only.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — the Target/CouplingMap caveat paragraph ("Jednak target może zawierać instrukcje działające na więcej niż dwóch Qubitach…") and both table notes on readout error/length keep every hedge and the "tylko widok informacji" qualifier intact, register is consistently informal ("jeśli korzystasz z providera", "musisz wprowadzić"). One genuine slip: the English idiom "will be removed from service" is calqued as "zostanie usunięta z serwisu", which in Polish reads as "removed from the website/service centre" rather than "withdrawn from use" — a Polish engineer would write "zostanie wycofana z użycia". Secondary nit: the note block shifts tense between "zawsze używał" (past) and "korzysta … od wersji 0.13" (present) where the English keeps parallel present-perfect; harmless but slightly uneven.

examples.source: has been deprecated and will be removed from service
examples.translation: została oznaczona jako przestarzała i zostanie usunięta z serwisu
examples.suggested: została oznaczona jako przestarzała i zostanie wycofana z użycia
examples.why: "serwis" in Polish means a website or a repair/service centre; the English "removed from service" means withdrawn from use. Calqued idiom that a native technical writer would not use.

examples.source: The qiskit_ibm_runtime package has always used BackendV2, and qiskit_aer has used BackendV2 since version 0.13.
examples.translation: Pakiet `qiskit_ibm_runtime` zawsze używał `BackendV2`, a `qiskit_aer` korzysta z `BackendV2` od wersji 0.13.
examples.suggested: Pakiet `qiskit_ibm_runtime` zawsze korzystał z `BackendV2`, a `qiskit_aer` korzysta z niego od wersji 0.13.
examples.why: Unnecessary verb/tense switch (używał → korzysta) across a parallel construction the English keeps symmetric; meaning is unaffected.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout and idiomatic; domain terms are the correct Polish conventions and consistent (projective measurements→"pomiary rzutowe", superdense coding→"supergęste kodowanie", entanglement→"splątanie kwantowe", CHSH game→"gra CHSH (zwana również nierównością CHSH)"), the Credly privacy paragraph is rendered completely with no dropped caveat, and informal "ty" holds ("Witaj w kursie…", "twoje dane osobowe"); only nit is the slightly stiff "układów pojedynczych i wielokrotnych" for "single and multiple systems", which does not mislead.

Meaning is faithful throughout — every card description maps 1:1 to the English (ZNE "extrapolando os resultados de valores esperados ruidosos para o limite de ruído zero", OBP "ao custo de mais medições de operadores", PEC "ao custo de maior sobrecarga do que outras técnicas como ZNE"), no drift, no dropped caveat, no hallucinated claim. Two isolated terminology slips, neither misleading: PEC's "unbiased estimate" is rendered "estimativa imparcial", whereas the statistical convention in Portuguese is "estimativa não enviesada" (or "não tendenciosa") — "imparcial" carries the everyday sense of "fair/neutral" rather than the technical sense of zero bias; and Pauli twirling's "noise-tailoring technique" becomes "técnica de ajuste de ruído", which flattens the sense of *shaping/tailoring* the noise into a target channel ("adaptação/moldagem do ruído" would keep it). Register is fine (informal "ajudá-lo", "seu fluxo de trabalho") and section headings read naturally.

examples.source: Returns an unbiased estimate of the expectation value
examples.translation: Retorna uma estimativa imparcial do valor esperado
examples.suggested: Retorna uma estimativa não enviesada do valor esperado
examples.why: 'Unbiased' is a statistical term of art; Portuguese convention is 'não enviesada'/'não tendenciosa'. 'Imparcial' reads as 'fair/neutral' in ordinary usage and loses the zero-bias meaning that is the whole point of PEC's trade-off against ZNE.

examples.source: Noise-tailoring technique that transforms any noise channel into a Pauli channel
examples.translation: Técnica de ajuste de ruído que transforma qualquer canal de ruído em um canal de Pauli
examples.suggested: Técnica de adaptação (moldagem) do ruído que transforma qualquer canal de ruído em um canal de Pauli
examples.why: 'Ajuste' suggests tuning a knob; 'noise tailoring' means reshaping the noise channel into a prescribed form, which is exactly what the rest of the sentence describes. The chosen word slightly flattens the pedagogical intuition without contradicting it.

editor_note: Pure bibliography (136 IEEE-style entries): the only translatable prose is the frontmatter and two headings, all faithful and natural — "Works cited" → "Obras citadas", "Full list of references" → "Lista completa de referências", and the description "Este documento fornece uma lista de todas as citações utilizadas no curso." All 136 reference entries (titles, venues, DOIs, Wikipedia URLs, e.g. [39] Gidney/Ekerå "How to factor 2048 bit RSA integers in 8 hours using 20 million noisy qubits") are correctly left untranslated, which is the right convention for citations; entry numbering, ordering, and figures are identical to the English. No semantic drift or hallucination possible or present.

Meaning is faithful throughout — no drift, no hallucination, no dropped caveats: the sampling-overhead trade-off ("Por outro lado, o corte resulta na necessidade de amostragem extra... sobrecarga de amostragem de $9^3$"), the "reduzida em mais da metade" depth claim, and the Step 2/Step 4 attribution of gate cutting all match the English exactly, and core terms (qubit, valor de expectativa, observáveis, subexperimentos, backend, transpilado, Primitiva Sampler) are correct and consistent. Two non-misleading slips: a plain typo "decomor" for "decompor" in the QPDBasis paragraph (Step 2), and "preset pass manager" rendered as "gerenciador de passos predefinido", where "passos" collides with the tutorial's own "Passo 1/2/3/4" headings — a Portuguese quantum engineer would write "gerenciador de passes (pass manager)" to keep transpiler passes distinct from the pattern's steps. Neither changes what a learner takes away.

examples.source: The `QPDBasis` object contains information about how to decompose the cut gates into single-qubit operations.
examples.translation: O objeto `QPDBasis` contém informações sobre como decomor as portas cortadas em operações de um único qubit.
examples.suggested: O objeto `QPDBasis` contém informações sobre como decompor as portas cortadas em operações de um único qubit.
examples.why: "decomor" is not a Portuguese word — a dropped-syllable typo for "decompor". Purely mechanical; meaning is still recoverable.

examples.source: We choose `optimization_level=3`, which is the highest level of optimization available with a preset pass manager.
examples.translation: Escolhemos `optimization_level=3`, que é o nível mais alto de otimização disponível com um gerenciador de passos predefinido.
examples.suggested: ...disponível com um gerenciador de passes (pass manager) predefinido.
examples.why: Transpiler "passes" are conventionally "passes" in PT quantum docs, not "passos"; here "passos" also clashes with the file's own "Passo 1–4" section headings, blurring two distinct concepts. Defensible but suboptimal — does not mislead about the code.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout and reads natively; core terms are correct and consistent (expectation values → "valores esperados", observables → "observáveis", error suppression/mitigation → "supressão e mitigação de erros", output register → "registrador de saída"), the Executor beta caveat ("interface de nível mais baixo que oferece maior visibilidade e controle sem sacrificar o desempenho") preserves the English hedging exactly, and no claim is added, softened, or inverted.

Meaning is faithful throughout — the entanglement proof by contradiction, the partial-measurement/normalization argument, the 5-tuple ket-permutation example, and the GHZ/W measurement probabilities (2/3, 1/3, |100⟩) all track the English exactly, and terminology is correct and consistent (estado produto, produto tensorial, emaranhamento, medição na base padrão, matriz de densidade, conjugado transposto, vetor unitário). Three isolated slips, none misleading: line ~82 renders "alternatively written" as "escrito alternadamente" (= in alternation), while lines 576/1057 correctly use "alternativamente" for the same idea; line ~568 turns "the remaining systems are left alone" into "os sistemas restantes são deixados em repouso" ("left at rest"), a physics-loaded reading of a phrase that just means unmeasured; and lines ~779/781 pair "não é particularmente especial" with "dada a forma especial da matriz U", where the English said "particular form" — the near-repetition of "especial" reads self-contradictory. Also minor: line ~385 pre-empts "colapsa para" where the English still says "becomes" and only introduces the quoted "collapses" in the following sentence.

examples.source: The tensor product |φ⟩ ⊗ |ψ⟩, which may alternatively be written as |φ⟩|ψ⟩ or as |φ⊗ψ⟩
examples.translation: O produto tensorial $\vert \phi \rangle \otimes \vert \psi \rangle,$ que pode ser escrito alternadamente como ...
examples.suggested: que pode ser escrito alternativamente como
examples.why: "alternadamente" means "in alternation / taking turns", not "alternatively". The file itself uses the correct "alternativamente" at lines 576 and 1057, so this is also an internal inconsistency.

examples.source: We'll consider the situation in which the first and third systems are measured, and the remaining systems are left alone.
examples.translation: Vamos considerar a situação em que o primeiro e o terceiro sistemas são medidos e os sistemas restantes são deixados em repouso.
examples.suggested: ... e os sistemas restantes não são tocados (ou: são deixados intactos).
examples.why: "deixados em repouso" reads as "left at rest", a physical-state claim, in a passage whose only point is that those systems are not measured. A native technical writer would say "não são tocados"/"deixados intactos".

examples.source: This unitary matrix isn't special, it's just an example. ... which is made simpler in this case given the particular form of the matrix U.
examples.translation: Essa matriz unitária não é particularmente especial, é apenas um exemplo. ... o que neste caso é mais fácil, dada a forma especial da matriz $U.$
examples.suggested: ... dada a forma particular da matriz $U$.
examples.why: The English contrasts "not special" with "the particular form"; rendering both with "especial" two sentences apart makes the text look like it contradicts itself. "forma particular" restores the intended sense.

editor_note: Faithful, natural pt-BR technical prose with consistent informal "você"; the option semantics survive intact (`plans_preference` → "Isso é ignorado se `instance` for especificado", `overwrite` → "Defina esse valor como `True` para atualizar suas credenciais padrão"), and Example 2's constraint is preserved exactly ("buscará apenas instâncias premium na região da UE disponíveis para a conta"), with domain terms correctly kept in English (CRN, token, tags, Qiskit Runtime, chave de API).

Meaning is faithful throughout — I checked the delicate passages in the last 40% (the fourth solution being only the fifth most probable, the "quatro cortes" of the wrong solution, the three error sources, the 32 bitstrings / top-five claim) and none drift; the informal "você" convention holds and core terms (Hamiltoniano misturador, estado fundamental, ansatz, transpilar/transpilador, backend, shots, bitstring, portas nativas) are correct and consistent. The defects are isolated slips, not meaning problems: "transpilation passes" is rendered "passagens de transpilação" (passages/tickets) instead of the conventional "passes de transpilação"; "two-local Pauli ZZ operators" is calqued as "operadores ZZ de Pauli de dois locais" rather than "dois-local"/"de dois corpos". Two naturalness slips: line ~138 drops the correlative verb ("então em teoria, quanto mais preciso o resultado" — needs "mais preciso será o resultado"), and line ~197 mixes person ("tudo que precisava fazer era girar meu corte" for "all I had to do was rotate my cut"). None of these would mislead a learner.

examples.source: The Qiskit library offers a series of transpilation passes that cater to a wide range of circuit transformations.
examples.translation: A biblioteca Qiskit oferece uma série de passagens de transpilação que atendem a uma ampla gama de transformações de circuito.
examples.suggested: A biblioteca Qiskit oferece uma série de passes de transpilação que atendem a uma ampla gama de transformações de circuito.
examples.why: "Passagem" means a passage/ticket; the Qiskit compiler concept is kept as "pass" (passe) in Portuguese Qiskit usage. Literal-but-wrong rendering of a domain term.

examples.source: In general, the more layers or steps we include, the closer we will be to continuous time evolution, like in QAA, so in theory, the more accurate the result will be.
examples.translation: Em geral, quanto mais camadas ou passos incluirmos, mais próximos estaremos da evolução contínua do tempo, como no QAA, então em teoria, quanto mais preciso o resultado.
examples.suggested: ... como no QAA — portanto, em teoria, mais preciso será o resultado.
examples.why: The second correlative clause is left verbless ("quanto mais preciso o resultado"), which is ungrammatical in Portuguese; a native technical writer would close it with "mais preciso será o resultado".

examples.source: we need to rewrite it in terms of two-local Pauli ZZ operators
examples.translation: precisamos reescrevê-lo em termos de operadores ZZ de Pauli de dois locais
examples.suggested: precisamos reescrevê-lo em termos de operadores ZZ de Pauli dois-local (de dois corpos)
examples.why: "de dois locais" reads as "in two places"; "two-local" is a physics term of art (support on two qubits), normally kept as "dois-local" or rendered "de dois corpos".

examples.source: You can see that all I had to do was rotate my cut to include these new points.
examples.translation: Você pode ver que tudo que precisava fazer era girar meu corte para incluir esses novos pontos.
examples.suggested: Você pode ver que tudo o que eu precisei fazer foi girar meu corte para incluir esses novos pontos.
examples.why: Dropping the subject pronoun makes "precisava fazer" read as second person ("all you had to do") while the possessive stays first person ("meu corte"), producing a person clash the English does not have.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout and reads as native technical Portuguese; core terms are correct and consistent (qubit→qubit, gate→porta, transpile→transpilar, backend→backend, layout/routing→Layout/Roteamento, fidelity→fidelidade), and the load-bearing claims survive intact — "Isso produz os mesmos resultados que o nível de otimização 1. Note que aumentar o nível de otimização nem sempre faz diferença" and "qualquer porta de dois qubits pode ser implementada usando no máximo três portas ECR" both keep the exact hedge and quantity of the English, as does the approximation caveat ("é um circuito aproximado") and the 127→2 active-qubit reduction.

Meaning is faithful end-to-end — the retirement table, the CRN caveat ("Dacă nu este specificată o instanță, toate Backend-urile și joburile din toate instanțele contului tău sunt disponibile"), the Osprey "aproape de patru ori mai mare decât Eagle, cu 433 de qubiți" and the deprecated-provider note all match the English with no inversion or dropped nuance, and informal "tu" holds throughout. Two hardware-terminology slips in the "Familii de procesoare retrase" list: semiconductor "packaging" is rendered literally as "ambalajul dispozitivelor" / "tehnologii de ambalare" (Romanian for wrapping/boxing goods) where the field says "încapsulare" / "tehnologii de încapsulare"; and "heavy-hexagonal qubit layout" becomes "layout de qubiți hexagonal greu", which both mistranslates the lattice name (heavy-hex → "hexagonală grea", or kept as "heavy-hex") and, by masculine agreement, attaches "hexagonal greu" to "layout" rather than to the lattice. Neither misleads on a factual claim, so this stays MINOR rather than FAIL.

examples.source: The larger chip sizes required further enhancements to device packaging ... The Eagle processor family incorporated more scalable packaging technologies than previous generations.
examples.translation: Dimensiunile mai mari ale cipurilor au necesitat îmbunătățiri suplimentare ale ambalajului dispozitivelor ... Familia de procesoare Eagle a încorporat tehnologii de ambalare mai scalabile decât generațiile anterioare.
examples.suggested: ... îmbunătățiri suplimentare ale încapsulării dispozitivelor ... a încorporat tehnologii de încapsulare mai scalabile ...
examples.why: In chip engineering, "packaging" is încapsulare (the die/carrier assembly). "Ambalaj/ambalare" means shipping wrapping in Romanian, so a learner reads it as how the device is boxed up rather than as a chip-level integration technology. It is used this way twice, consistently wrong.

examples.source: **Hummingbird**: Using a heavy-hexagonal qubit layout, the Hummingbird family allowed up to 65 qubits.
examples.translation: **Hummingbird**: Folosind un layout de qubiți hexagonal greu, familia Hummingbird a permis până la 65 de qubiți.
examples.suggested: **Hummingbird**: Folosind o rețea de qubiți hexagonală grea (heavy-hex), familia Hummingbird a permis până la 65 de qubiți.
examples.why: "heavy-hexagonal" is the name of IBM's heavy-hex lattice, not a description of weight; "hexagonal greu" reads as "a heavy hexagon" and, being masculine, grammatically qualifies "layout" instead of the qubit lattice. The conventional term (rețea hexagonală grea / heavy-hex) is lost.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout, including the tricky trailing comparisons ("$|0111\rangle$ este observat cu cea mai mică probabilitate, iar probabilitatea ... a scăzut și mai mult" vs. the 3-iteration "a scăzut ușor"), the preserved source quirk "patru componente principale" over a three-item list, and correct informal "tu" ("Reține că trebuie să fii atent la ordinea qubiților"); terminology is consistent (qubit/qubiți, operatorul de difuzie, amplificarea amplitudinii, transpilare, porțile de bază native).

The prose is largely fluent and the long Background/self-consistent-recovery paragraphs are faithful (spin-up→jumătatea dreaptă / spin-down→jumătatea stângă is correctly preserved), but there are two genuine meaning defects plus term splits. In "Vizualizarea rezultatelor" the chemistry term "spatial orbital" is rendered as "orbite spațiale" / "primele cinci orbite" — "orbită" is an orbit (Bohr-style), not an orbital; the rest of the file correctly says "orbitalele" / "ocupanțele orbitale", so this both misteaches and contradicts the file's own convention. In "Pași următori", "how to use the more efficient Dice software for diagonalization" becomes "cum să folosești software-ul Dice mai eficient pentru diagonalizare", which reads as "how to use Dice more efficiently" — the comparative is detached from Dice and attached to the user's usage, inverting the claim. Additional within-file splits: "samples" is eșantioane in the title/Background but mostre in Steps 3–4 and the plot discussion; "heavy-hex" appears both as the calque "rețea hex grea" and untranslated as "heavy-hex"/"hartă de cuplare heavy-hex". Informal "tu" register holds throughout; "consulta" for "consultă" appears twice (typo, cosmetic).

examples.source: The second plot shows the average occupancy of each spatial orbital after the final iteration. We can see that both the spin-up and spin-down electrons occupy the first five orbitals with high probability in our solutions.
examples.translation: Al doilea grafic arată ocupanța medie a fiecărei orbite spațiale după iterația finală. Putem observa că atât electronii cu spin-up, cât și cei cu spin-down ocupă primele cinci orbite cu probabilitate ridicată în soluțiile noastre.
examples.suggested: Al doilea grafic arată ocupanța medie a fiecărui orbital spațial după iterația finală. Putem observa că atât electronii cu spin-up, cât și cei cu spin-down ocupă primii cinci orbitali cu probabilitate ridicată în soluțiile noastre.
examples.why: 'orbită' = orbit (classical/Bohr trajectory); the quantum-chemistry term is 'orbital' (m., pl. 'orbitali'). The rest of the file already uses 'orbitalele'/'ocupanțele orbitale', so this paragraph is both wrong and inconsistent, and it is exactly the paragraph teaching the reader how to read the occupancy plot.

examples.source: [Scale SQD chemistry workflows with Dice solver] - a page showing how to use the more efficient Dice software for diagonalization
examples.translation: [Extinde fluxurile de lucru SQD de chimie cu soluționatorul Dice] - o pagină care arată cum să folosești software-ul Dice mai eficient pentru diagonalizare
examples.suggested: ... - o pagină care arată cum să folosești software-ul Dice, mai eficient, pentru diagonalizare  (or: ... cum să folosești software-ul mai eficient Dice pentru diagonalizare)
examples.why: 'mai eficient' lands as an adverb modifying 'să folosești', so the Romanian promises a page about using Dice more efficiently. The English says Dice itself is the more efficient software — the whole reason for the recommendation.

examples.source: collect samples for ground state energy estimation / iteratively refine the noisy quantum samples / increasing the number of samples per batch
examples.translation: să colectăm mostre pentru estimarea energiei stării fundamentale / a rafina iterativ mostrele cuantice zgomotoase / mărind numărul de mostre per lot
examples.suggested: eșantioane (…să colectăm eșantioane… / eșantioanele cuantice cu zgomot… / numărul de eșantioane per lot)
examples.why: The title, Background and Steps 1–2 consistently use 'eșantioane' (and 'a eșantiona', 'bazată pe eșantioane'); Steps 3–4 and the plot discussion switch to 'mostre'. Same concept, two words within one tutorial — a reader cannot tell whether 'mostre' is something else.

examples.source: IBM hardware has a heavy-hex lattice qubit topology / we'll create a generic backend with a heavy-hex coupling map
examples.translation: Hardware-ul IBM are o topologie de qubiți cu rețea hex grea / vom crea un backend generic cu o hartă de cuplare heavy-hex
examples.suggested: Use 'heavy-hex' in both: 'o topologie de qubiți cu rețea heavy-hex' / 'o hartă de cuplare heavy-hex'
examples.why: 'hex grea' is a literal calque of a term the Romanian-speaking field keeps in English (and which the code itself uses as connectivity="heavy-hex"); the file already keeps 'heavy-hex' 100 lines later, so the calque is both unnatural and self-inconsistent.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — no drift, no dropped caveat, no hallucination; the noise-drift caveat ("zgomotul de pe dispozitive poate fluctua și deriva, iar zgomotul învățat s-ar putea să nu fie precis la momentul la care se face estimarea"), the optimality claim ("nicio metodă nu poate obține un overhead de măsurare mai mic") and the noiseless-value note all land exactly as in the English, and the informal "tu" convention holds ("poți calcula", "Autentifică-te", "Contactează"). Two naturalness slips: the compound "error-mitigated" is rendered as a possessive genitive that a Romanian technical writer would not write — "valori de așteptare atenuate ale erorilor" (Description) and "Estimarea atenuată a erorii unui observabil O" (image caption) both literally read "expectation values/estimation mitigated OF the errors" rather than "cu erori atenuate". Also "estimatori nebiasați" is a calque; the standard Romanian statistical term is "estimatori nedeplasați". Neither misleads a learner; core terminology (valori de așteptare, observabili, rețele tensoriale, straturi, porți cu doi qubiți, măsurători complet informaționale / IC-POVM) is correct and consistent within the file.

examples.source: The TEM function allows you to obtain error-mitigated expectation values for multiple observables on a quantum circuit
examples.translation: Funcția TEM îți permite să obții valori de așteptare atenuate ale erorilor pentru mai mulți observabili pe un Circuit cuantic
examples.suggested: Funcția TEM îți permite să obții valori de așteptare cu erori atenuate pentru mai mulți observabili pe un circuit cuantic
examples.why: The genitive 'atenuate ale erorilor' reads as if the expectation values themselves are mitigated 'of the errors'; the English compound 'error-mitigated' modifies the values via the errors being corrected. Meaning survives, but the phrasing is calqued and no native technical writer would use it.

examples.source: to obtain unbiased estimators for the observables
examples.translation: pentru a obține estimatori nebiasați ai observabililor
examples.suggested: pentru a obține estimatori nedeplasați ai observabililor
examples.why: 'nebiasat' is an ad-hoc calque of 'unbiased'; the conventional Romanian statistics term is 'estimator nedeplasat' (occasionally 'nedistorsionat'). Not misleading, but the wrong register for a domain term.

examples.source: Error-mitigated estimation of an observable O via post-processing measurement outcomes
examples.translation: Estimarea atenuată a erorii unui observabil O prin post-procesarea rezultatelor de măsurare
examples.suggested: Estimarea cu erori atenuate a unui observabil O prin post-procesarea rezultatelor de măsurare
examples.why: Same calqued pattern as above, and here it shifts the reading toward 'the mitigated estimation of the error of an observable O' — it is the estimation that is error-mitigated, not the error that is estimated. In an image caption this is low-impact, but it is the same defect repeated.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout, including the dense last 40% (the block-matrix M argument, "primele n coloane ale matricei M sunt ortonormate", and the closing "X se modifică exact cum s-ar fi modificat dacă măsurătoarea proiectivă ... ar fi fost efectuată direct pe X"); domain terms are correct and consistent (produs interior, mulțime ortonormată / bază ortonormată, matrice hermitiene / idempotente, măsurătoare proiectivă, matrice unitară), informal "tu" holds ("Observă că norma euclidiană..."), and the only stylistic wobbles — "definititudine pozitivă" for positive definiteness and "procesul de ortonormare Gram–Schmidt" for the orthogonalization process — are isolated and do not mislead.

Meaning is faithful throughout, including the passages most likely to break: the `dt` heuristic ("este preferabil, până la un punct, să subestimăm această valoare mai degrabă decât să o supraestimăm") preserves the direction of the recommendation, and the whole Appendix bound derivation (upper-bounding the first term by δ, lower-bounding the denominator by |γ₀|², the Rayleigh–Ritz variational argument, and "dacă δ<E₁−E₀ atunci termenul δ dispare complet") tracks the English exactly. The defects are register and terminology: the informal "tu" convention holds for most of the file ("Consultă Anexa", "Alege o stare de referință", "consideră următorul circuit") but breaks twice in the Appendix with formal-plural "rețineți" (line ~1034) and "Consultați [\[2\]]" (line ~1038). "ancilla qubit" is rendered as "Qubit ansilă" — "ansilă" is not a Romanian word; the field uses "qubit auxiliar" or keeps "ancilla". Also one within-file split: "problemă generalizată a valorilor proprii" (line ~202) vs "problema valorilor proprii generalizate" (line ~894), and a missing diacritic in "Putem margini superior" (line ~1016) where the surrounding text correctly uses "mărginim inferior"/"Mărginind". None of these mislead a learner.

examples.source: Also note that if $\delta<E_1-E_0$ then the $\delta$ term actually goes away entirely in the above bound.
examples.translation: De asemenea, rețineți că dacă $\delta<E_1-E_0$ atunci termenul $\delta$ dispare complet în marginea de mai sus.
examples.suggested: De asemenea, reține că dacă $\delta<E_1-E_0$ atunci termenul $\delta$ dispare complet în marginea de mai sus.
examples.why: Formal-plural imperative breaks the informal "tu" convention the rest of the file holds ("Consultă Anexa", "Alege o stare de referință"). Same slip at line ~1038 with "Consultați [\[2\]]".

examples.source: The top wire in the Hadamard test circuit is an ancilla qubit which is measured either in the X or Y basis
examples.translation: Firul de sus din circuitul testului Hadamard este un Qubit ansilă care este măsurat fie în baza X, fie în baza Y
examples.suggested: Firul de sus din circuitul testului Hadamard este un qubit auxiliar care este măsurat fie în baza X, fie în baza Y
examples.why: "ansilă" is a phonetic calque of "ancilla" that is not a Romanian word; Romanian quantum writing uses "qubit auxiliar" or keeps "ancilla" unadapted. Recurs in the same paragraph ("starea Qubitului ansilă").

examples.source: Finally, we can solve the generalized eigenvalue problem for $\tilde{H}$
examples.translation: În final, putem rezolva problema valorilor proprii generalizate pentru $\tilde{H}$
examples.suggested: În final, putem rezolva problema generalizată a valorilor proprii pentru $\tilde{H}$
examples.why: Within-file split: line ~202 correctly renders the term as "problemă generalizată a valorilor proprii" (the problem is generalized), whereas this instance reads as "generalized eigenvalues" — the adjective attaches to the wrong noun.

examples.source: where the first step follows because $E_k-E_0\le\delta$ for every $E_k$ in the sum
examples.translation: unde primul pas urmează deoarece $E_k-E_0\le\delta$ pentru fiecare $E_k$ din sumă
examples.suggested: unde primul pas rezultă din faptul că $E_k-E_0\le\delta$ pentru fiecare $E_k$ din sumă
examples.why: "urmează deoarece" is a literal calque of "follows because"; in Romanian mathematical prose "urmează" means "comes next", not "follows logically" — a native writer uses "rezultă".

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — no drift, no dropped caveats, no hallucination; the tricky "unless you have workloads that don't have all inputs ready" is correctly inverted to "dacă nu ai sarcini de lucru cărora le lipsesc intrările la început", and the drift/convergence passage in the variational-algorithm example keeps the English's causal logic intact. Informal "tu" holds everywhere (folosește, ia în considerare, îți ajustezi). Three isolated surface slips remain: a typo "reruezi" (→ "rerulezi") in the batch bullet; a grammatical/register error "este benefic că rulează cât mai aproape în timp" where Romanian requires the subjunctive "să ruleze"; and "wall-clock time" calqued twice as "timpul de ceas de perete", which no Romanian technical writer would use ("timp real"/"durata efectivă", or keep "wall-clock"). None of these mislead a learner.

examples.source: if one job fails, you can re-submit it instead of re-running the entire workload
examples.translation: dacă un job eșuează, îl poți retrimite în loc să reruezi întreaga sarcină de lucru
examples.suggested: … în loc să rulezi din nou întreaga sarcină de lucru
examples.why: "reruezi" is a typo/non-word; the verb is "rerulezi", and idiomatic Romanian prefers "să rulezi din nou".

examples.source: it's beneficial that they run as closely together as possible
examples.translation: este benefic că rulează cât mai aproape în timp
examples.suggested: este util ca ele să ruleze cât mai apropiat în timp
examples.why: "benefic că rulează" is ungrammatical here — the construction requires the subjunctive "să ruleze"; as written it reads as asserting a fact rather than stating a desirable condition.

examples.source: wall-clock time
examples.translation: timpul de ceas de perete
examples.suggested: timpul real (wall-clock)
examples.why: Literal calque; Romanian technical prose says "timp real"/"durata efectivă" or keeps the English term — "ceas de perete" reads as raw MT.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout, including the error-prone qubit-orientation bookkeeping ("lasă qubit-ul din dreapta/sus neschimbat și aplică XOR ... pe qubit-ul din stânga/jos"), the parity lower-bound argument, and the eigenvector/eigenvalue passage ("vector propriu al matricei X cu valoarea proprie -1"); terminology is conventional and consistent (qubit, poartă, interogare, phase kickback kept in English) and the informal "tu" holds ("Observă că...").

Meaning is broadly faithful — the MVC penalty derivation, the "Number of qubits is not a hard limitation" caveat, and the weighted-cost explanation in "3. ดูผลลัพธ์" all survive intact, and the informal register holds throughout (no ครับ/ค่ะ). Three real slips: (1) "under the hood" is calqued literally as "ภายใต้ฝาครอบ", which a Thai technical writer would never write (natural: "เบื้องหลัง" — which the translator in fact uses correctly one paragraph later at "เบื้องหลัง pipeline ของ Solver"); (2) "error suppression at the hardware level" becomes "การกดสัญญาณรบกวนในระดับ hardware" (noise suppression), narrowing error→noise even though the same sentence's "ลด error" keeps "error"; (3) "representation" is rendered three times as "การแสดงผล" ("displaying output") in the Accepted-problem-formats bullets, where the sense is "การแทน(ปัญหา)ด้วย…" — the wrong noun, though context keeps it from misleading. None of these invert a condition, swap a quantity, or add unsourced claims.

examples.source: leverages Fire Opal's Performance Management under the hood
examples.translation: ใช้ประโยชน์จาก Fire Opal's Performance Management ภายใต้ฝาครอบ
examples.suggested: ใช้ประโยชน์จาก Fire Opal's Performance Management อยู่เบื้องหลัง
examples.why: 'ภายใต้ฝาครอบ' is a word-for-word calque of the car-hood idiom and reads as MT; the file itself uses the idiomatic 'เบื้องหลัง' correctly in the very next section.

examples.source: from error suppression at the hardware level to efficient problem mapping
examples.translation: ตั้งแต่การกดสัญญาณรบกวนในระดับ hardware ไปจนถึงการ mapping ปัญหาที่มีประสิทธิภาพ
examples.suggested: ตั้งแต่การกด error ในระดับ hardware ไปจนถึงการ mapping ปัญหาที่มีประสิทธิภาพ
examples.why: 'error suppression' is a specific Q-CTRL pipeline stage; rendering it as 'การกดสัญญาณรบกวน' (noise suppression) narrows the claim and is inconsistent with 'ลด error' kept in the same sentence.

examples.source: Polynomial expression representation of an objective function. / Graph representation of a specific problem type. / Spin chain representation of a specific problem.
examples.translation: การแสดงผลด้วย Polynomial expression ของ objective function / การแสดงผลด้วย Graph ของ problem type เฉพาะ / การแสดงผลด้วย Spin chain ของปัญหาเฉพาะ
examples.suggested: การแทน objective function ด้วย Polynomial expression / การแทนปัญหาเฉพาะประเภทด้วย Graph / การแทนปัญหาเฉพาะด้วย Spin chain
examples.why: 'การแสดงผล' means 'displaying output', not 'representation'; the conventional Thai for representing a problem in a given form is 'การแทน…ด้วย…'. Repeated across all three bullets.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful end-to-end — the variational-theorem derivation, the ≥ λ₀ bound, the "equality only if ∃ θ* with |ψ(θ*)⟩ = |φ₀⟩" caveat, and the closing "if finite upper bounds exist, swap lower for upper bounds" generalization are all rendered correctly, so nothing here misleads a learner. Two word-choice slips recur: "intuition" is rendered as แรงจูงใจ ("motivation") both in the heading แรงจูงใจทางคณิตศาสตร์สำหรับพลังงานและ ground states and in the lead-in เรามาสำรวจแรงจูงใจทางคณิตศาสตร์กัน — Thai technical writing would use สัญชาตญาณเชิงคณิตศาสตร์ / ความเข้าใจเชิงสัญชาตญาณ; and "argument" (as in a mathematical argument/derivation) is rendered ข้อโต้แย้ง ("objection/dispute") twice (ข้อโต้แย้งข้างต้นใช้กับสถานะควอนตัม…, ข้อโต้แย้งทางคณิตศาสตร์เดียวกัน), where การให้เหตุผล is the natural term. Also "reference operator unitary $U_R$" keeps English word order instead of ตัวดำเนินการอ้างอิงแบบยูนิแทรี. Terminology is otherwise consistent (ansatz/optimizer/Hamiltonian/observable kept English, cost function → ฟังก์ชันต้นทุน throughout), and the informal register holds with no ครับ/ค่ะ.

examples.source: Before going into its full statement, let us explore some of the mathematical intuition behind it. / ### Mathematical intuition for energy and ground states
examples.translation: ก่อนที่จะเข้าสู่เนื้อหาทั้งหมด เรามาสำรวจแรงจูงใจทางคณิตศาสตร์กัน / ### แรงจูงใจทางคณิตศาสตร์สำหรับพลังงานและ ground states
examples.suggested: เรามาสำรวจความเข้าใจเชิงสัญชาตญาณทางคณิตศาสตร์เบื้องหลังกันก่อน / ### สัญชาตญาณเชิงคณิตศาสตร์สำหรับพลังงานและ ground states
examples.why: แรงจูงใจ means 'motivation', not 'intuition'; the pedagogical promise of the section (build intuition before the formal statement) is blurred. 'some of the' is also dropped.

examples.source: The above argument applies to any valid (normalized) quantum state … / the same mathematical arguments could be made for maximizing eigenvalues
examples.translation: ข้อโต้แย้งข้างต้นใช้กับสถานะควอนตัมที่ถูกต้อง (normalized) ใด ๆ … / สามารถใช้ข้อโต้แย้งทางคณิตศาสตร์เดียวกันสำหรับการเพิ่มค่าเฉพาะให้สูงสุด
examples.suggested: การให้เหตุผลข้างต้นใช้ได้กับ… / สามารถใช้การให้เหตุผลทางคณิตศาสตร์เดียวกัน…
examples.why: ข้อโต้แย้ง reads as 'objection/dispute' in Thai; for a derivation a native technical writer uses การให้เหตุผล/ข้อพิสูจน์. Reads as MT literalism.

examples.source: This transformation is represented by the application of a unitary reference operator $U_R$ on the default state
examples.translation: การแปลงนี้แสดงโดยการใช้ reference operator unitary $U_R$ กับสถานะดีฟอลต์
examples.suggested: การแปลงนี้แสดงด้วยการกระทำของตัวดำเนินการอ้างอิงแบบยูนิแทรี $U_R$ บนสถานะดีฟอลต์
examples.why: 'reference operator unitary' keeps English constituents in a non-Thai order and is not a term any Thai quantum writer would produce.

editor_note: Meaning-faithful throughout and reads as native informal Thai technical prose (no ครับ/ค่ะ); the key conceptual paragraph — "staged pass manager คือ pass manager ชนิดพิเศษที่แสดงถึงระดับของ abstraction ที่สูงกว่า pass manager ธรรมดา … ประกอบด้วย *pass managers* หลายตัว" — preserves the normal-vs-staged contrast and the emphasis exactly, and domain terms are consistently kept in English per convention (transpile/transpiler passes, pass manager, backend, basis set, circuit, single-qubit gates) with only Thai connective scaffolding around them.

Meaning is faithful throughout — I checked the load-bearing logic in the last 40% (the "compatible" non-destructive discussion at l.165–166, the V/V_a freedom at l.217–231, and the Kraus-condition note at l.270) and found no inversion, dropped caveat, or hallucinated claim; "necessary and sufficient" (จำเป็นและเพียงพอ, l.74) and "infinitely many" (อนันต์, l.165) both survive intact, and the informal register holds with no ครับ/ค่ะ. Three non-misleading slips: (1) "satisfying" is rendered as the non-word **สอบสนอง** twice (l.20 and l.238) — clearly a typo for สอดคล้อง/เป็นไปตาม; (2) "eigenvalues" is truncated to **ค่าเฉพาะ** (l.54) where the standard Thai term is ค่าลักษณะเฉพาะ; (3) matrix "entries" is rendered **รายการ** (l.137–138, l.141), which reads as "list item" rather than the conventional สมาชิก(ของเมทริกซ์). Consistency is otherwise good: การวัดแบบทำลาย/ไม่ทำลาย, ตั้งฉาก, and the kept-English unitary/orthonormal/block matrix are used uniformly. None of these would mislead a learner.

examples.source: a collection of positive semidefinite matrices satisfying
examples.translation: คอลเลกชันของเมทริกซ์ positive semidefinite ที่สอบสนอง
examples.suggested: คอลเลกชันของเมทริกซ์ positive semidefinite ที่สอดคล้องกับ
examples.why: สอบสนอง is not a Thai word — a typo/garbling of สอดคล้อง (or เป็นไปตาม). Appears in both the theorem setup and the Kraus-condition sentence.

examples.source: its eigenvalues must be nonnegative real numbers
examples.translation: ค่าเฉพาะของมันต้องเป็นจำนวนจริงที่ไม่ติดลบ
examples.suggested: ค่าลักษณะเฉพาะ (eigenvalue) ของมันต้องเป็นจำนวนจริงที่ไม่ติดลบ
examples.why: ค่าเฉพาะ is a truncation; the established Thai term for eigenvalue is ค่าลักษณะเฉพาะ. Not misleading here since the spectral decomposition is right beside it, but it is the wrong term.

examples.source: given by the diagonal entries of the reduced state
examples.translation: ให้โดยรายการแนวทแยงของ reduced state
examples.suggested: กำหนดโดยสมาชิกแนวทแยงของ reduced state
examples.why: รายการ reads as 'list item'; a Thai technical writer says สมาชิก (หรือ ตัวประกอบ) for matrix entries. Consistent within the file, so cosmetic rather than confusing.

editor_note: The file is short and otherwise faithful and natural: the caveat "this method does not return accurate results for dynamic circuits" survives intact as "เมธอดนี้ไม่คืนค่าที่ถูกต้องสำหรับ dynamic circuit", the stylesheet paragraph correctly keeps the "แต่ไม่จำเป็นต้องทำ" (but this is not required) qualifier, and terminology (เมธอด, circuit, Qubit, stylesheet, codebase) is consistent with informal register throughout. The one real slip is modality flattening in the intro: the English hedges "This visualization *could* help you to quickly spot idling time on qubits, *for example*", while the Thai asserts a flat capability — "การแสดงผลนี้ช่วยให้ระบุเวลาที่ Qubit ไม่ได้ทำงานได้อย่างรวดเร็ว" — dropping both the "could" hedge and the "for example" signal that idle-time spotting is just one illustrative use, not the tool's defined purpose. It does not mislead about how the API behaves, so it stays MINOR rather than FAIL.

examples.source: This visualization could help you to quickly spot idling time on qubits, for example.
examples.translation: การแสดงผลนี้ช่วยให้ระบุเวลาที่ Qubit ไม่ได้ทำงานได้อย่างรวดเร็ว
examples.suggested: ตัวอย่างเช่น การแสดงผลนี้อาจช่วยให้คุณระบุเวลาที่ Qubit ไม่ได้ทำงานได้อย่างรวดเร็ว
examples.why: The English hedges with 'could' and marks the use case as one example among others; the Thai turns it into a flat unhedged claim and drops 'for example', so a learner reads idle-time detection as the tool's stated function rather than an illustration.

editor_note: The prose is generally natural, informal (no ครับ/ค่ะ) and terminologically consistent (สับเปลี่ยนกันได้/commute, ถูกตรึง/fixed, น้ำหนักต่ำสุด/lowest weight, ระยะ/distance), but there is a genuine semantic inversion in the Shor-code section: EN "X⊗X⊗X is substituted for X" is rendered "$X\otimes X\otimes X$ ถูกแทนด้วย $X$", i.e. "XXX is replaced BY X" — the substitution direction is reversed, which breaks the whole point that the last two generators are the phase-flip repetition code with X promoted to XXX. Secondly, in the code-space-dimension proof, "these projections can be obtained by averaging the corresponding Pauli operations with the identity operation" becomes "การเฉลี่ย Pauli operations ที่สอดคล้องกับ identity operation", which parses as "averaging Pauli operations that correspond to the identity operation" rather than "averaging P_k together with I" — it no longer explains the formula Π_k = (I + P_k)/2. Thirdly, in the encoders section "a code space of a certain dimension" is rendered "code space ที่มีมิติหนึ่ง", readily read as "a one-dimensional code space" (the file itself uses หนึ่งมิติ for one-dimensional at line 238), contradicting the surrounding m-into-n encoding discussion.

examples.source: except that $X\otimes X\otimes X$ is substituted for $X,$ which is consistent with the fact that $X\otimes X\otimes X$ corresponds to an $X$ operation on logical qubits
examples.translation: ยกเว้นว่า $X\otimes X\otimes X$ ถูกแทนด้วย $X$ ซึ่งสอดคล้องกับข้อเท็จจริงที่ว่า $X\otimes X\otimes X$ สอดคล้องกับการดำเนินการ $X$ บน logical qubits
examples.suggested: ยกเว้นว่ามีการใช้ $X\otimes X\otimes X$ แทนที่ $X$
examples.why: Substitution direction inverted: Thai says XXX is replaced by X, the opposite of the English, which destroys the stated intuition that XXX plays the role of logical X.

examples.source: These projections can be obtained by averaging the corresponding Pauli operations with the identity operation as follows.
examples.translation: Projections เหล่านี้ได้จากการเฉลี่ย Pauli operations ที่สอดคล้องกับ identity operation ดังต่อไปนี้
examples.suggested: Projections เหล่านี้ได้จากการเฉลี่ย Pauli operation ที่สอดคล้องกันเข้ากับ identity operation ดังนี้
examples.why: 'with the identity' was turned into a relative clause ('Pauli operations that correspond to the identity'), so the sentence no longer explains why Π_k = (I + P_k)/2.

examples.source: A stabilizer code defines a code space of a certain dimension, and we have the freedom to use that code space however we choose
examples.translation: Stabilizer code กำหนด code space ที่มีมิติหนึ่ง และเรามีอิสระที่จะใช้ code space นั้นตามที่ต้องการ
examples.suggested: Stabilizer code กำหนด code space ที่มีมิติค่าหนึ่ง (มิติหนึ่งๆ) ...
examples.why: 'ที่มีมิติหนึ่ง' reads as 'one-dimensional' — the same wording family the file uses at line 238 for genuinely one-dimensional codes — contradicting the m-qubits-into-n encoding discussion that follows.

editor_note: The prose is otherwise native, fluent and faithful — the monitoring/calibrating/benchmarking narrative, the TLS caveat, the "ไม่เกินสองชั่วโมงต่อวัน" Heron figure, and the scheduling bullets (calibration cannot run while a job/session is active → reduced effective stability during long sessions) all match the English exactly. But the two-qubit crosstalk sentence inverts the source: English "run in batches of non-nearest-neighbor qubits to minimize crosstalk" became "รันเป็น batch ของ qubit ที่ไม่ใช่ non-nearest-neighbor", a double negation ("qubits that are NOT non-nearest-neighbor") that tells the learner the batches ARE nearest-neighbor qubits — the exact opposite of the stated crosstalk-avoidance rationale, so the sentence also contradicts its own purpose clause. Fix by dropping the stray "ที่ไม่ใช่".

examples.source: These calibrations are run in batches of non-nearest-neighbor qubits to minimize crosstalk.
examples.translation: การ calibrate เหล่านี้รันเป็น batch ของ qubit ที่ไม่ใช่ non-nearest-neighbor เพื่อลด crosstalk ให้น้อยที่สุด
examples.suggested: การ calibrate เหล่านี้รันเป็น batch ของ qubit ที่ไม่ได้อยู่ติดกัน (non-nearest-neighbor) เพื่อลด crosstalk ให้น้อยที่สุด
examples.why: 'ที่ไม่ใช่' + 'non-' is a double negative: it says the batches consist of qubits that are NOT non-nearest-neighbor, i.e. adjacent qubits — the inverse of the source and inconsistent with the crosstalk-minimization reason given in the same sentence.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout the heavy theory sections (VQE/SSVQE/VQD/QSR) and the domain terms are handled with a confident, consistent keep-English convention (ansatz, cost function, eigenvalue/eigenstate, orthogonal, expectation value, penalty term, transpile, Backend, Statevector Sampler/Estimator); the tricky VQD passages — "ค่าบวก β0 ควรมีค่ามากกว่า λ1−λ0" and the increasing-cost caveat at −6.00/4.02/5.61 — all track the English correctly. The one real defect is an omission at the very end: the Summary's closing paragraph ("These ideas can be adapted to form a custom variational algorithm that fits your problem. We encourage you to share your results with the community. The next lesson will explore how to use a variational algorithm to solve an application.") is entirely absent — the Thai file stops after the three bullets, dropping the call-to-action and the forward pointer to the next lesson. Two smaller slips: "we will apply the corresponding layout to the observable" is rendered "ใช้ layout ที่สอดคล้องกับ observable", which reads as "use the layout that corresponds to the observable" and inverts which object receives the layout; and "intrinsic properties" is rendered "คุณสมบัติเนื้อใน", a word a Thai technical writer would not use (คุณสมบัติภายใน / คุณสมบัติเฉพาะตัว). Register convention holds — informal, no ครับ/ค่ะ.

examples.source: These ideas can be adapted to form a custom variational algorithm that fits your problem. We encourage you to share your results with the community. The next lesson will explore how to use a variational algorithm to solve an application.
examples.translation: (missing — the Thai file ends after the third summary bullet)
examples.suggested: แนวคิดเหล่านี้สามารถนำไปปรับใช้เพื่อสร้างอัลกอริทึม variational แบบกำหนดเองที่เหมาะกับปัญหาของคุณ เราสนับสนุนให้คุณแบ่งปันผลลัพธ์กับชุมชน บทเรียนถัดไปจะสำรวจวิธีใช้อัลกอริทึม variational เพื่อแก้ปัญหาในการใช้งานจริง
examples.why: The entire closing paragraph of the Summary is dropped: the learner loses the takeaway that these ideas compose into a custom algorithm, the community call-to-action, and the pointer to the next lesson. This is content loss in the final section, not a style choice.

examples.source: We will transpile the circuit using the preset pass manager with optimization level 3, and we will apply the corresponding layout to the observable.
examples.translation: เราจะ transpile Circuit โดยใช้ preset pass manager ที่ optimization level 3 และใช้ layout ที่สอดคล้องกับ observable
examples.suggested: ... และจะนำ layout ที่ได้ไปใช้กับ observable
examples.why: The English applies the transpiled circuit's layout TO the observable (observable.apply_layout(layout=isa_ansatz.layout)). The Thai reads as 'use the layout that corresponds to the observable', reversing the direction of the operation relative to the code cell that immediately follows.

examples.source: ...while still capturing many of the inner workings and intrinsic properties of quantum computations that do not appear in simulation.
examples.translation: ขณะที่ยังคงจับเอาการทำงานภายในและคุณสมบัติเนื้อในของการคำนวณเชิงควอนตัมที่ไม่ปรากฏในการจำลอง
examples.suggested: ขณะที่ยังคงเก็บรายละเอียดการทำงานภายในและคุณสมบัติเฉพาะตัวของการคำนวณเชิงควอนตัมที่ไม่ปรากฏในการจำลอง
examples.why: 'คุณสมบัติเนื้อใน' is a literal calque of 'intrinsic'; no Thai technical writer would use เนื้อใน here (it evokes flesh/filling). คุณสมบัติภายใน or คุณสมบัติเฉพาะตัว is the conventional rendering. Meaning survives, but the phrase reads as MT.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — the admonition's "Hindi maaaring pamahalaan ang mga user hanggang hindi nila tinatanggap ang imbitasyon" correctly renders the English "until they accept", the two-project/three-user example setup maps 1:1, and no caveat or quantity is dropped. The slips are naturalness/terminology, not drift: "jobs" is rendered as "trabaho" (line 45), the ordinary-work sense, where the surrounding text keeps the platform term (`quantum-computing.job.delete`) in English — "job" should stay English; the same line's "Dapat na makakagawa ang mga user ng delete ng mga trabaho" is a non-idiomatic verb construction no native technical writer would use (natural: "Dapat na makapag-delete ang mga user ng mga job"). Also "environment" → "kapaligiran" (line 9) reads as physical/natural environment rather than a computing environment, and "mga mataas na antas na hakbang" (line 15) is a stiff calque of "high-level steps". Aspect is also inconsistent in the numbered scenario: most steps use present ("Gumagawa ang Cloud administrator"), but line 54 switches to past ("Inimbitahan…itinalaga nila"). Informal register holds — no po/opo.

examples.source: Users should be able to delete jobs.
examples.translation: Dapat na makakagawa ang mga user ng delete ng mga trabaho.
examples.suggested: Dapat na makapag-delete ang mga user ng mga job.
examples.why: "trabaho" is the ordinary-work sense of "job"; the platform term (cf. `quantum-computing.job.delete` in the same section) is kept in English elsewhere. "makakagawa ng delete" is also a non-idiomatic verb construction — Tagalog forms this with the mag- affix on the borrowed verb.

examples.source: gives instructions for users to access the environment
examples.translation: nagbibigay ng mga tagubilin para ma-access ng mga user ang kapaligiran
examples.suggested: …para ma-access ng mga user ang environment
examples.why: "kapaligiran" denotes a physical/natural environment; for a computing environment Tagalog technical prose keeps "environment".

examples.source: Follow these high-level steps to manage IBM Cloud users:
examples.translation: Sundin ang mga mataas na antas na hakbang na ito para pamahalaan ang mga IBM Cloud user:
examples.suggested: Sundin ang mga pangunahing hakbang na ito para pamahalaan ang mga IBM Cloud user:
examples.why: "mga mataas na antas na hakbang" is a word-for-word calque of "high-level steps" and reads as MT; "pangunahing hakbang" is what a native writer would use.

examples.source: The Cloud administrator invites cloud users to the appropriate project. Specifically, they invite and assign users…
examples.translation: Inimbitahan ng Cloud administrator ang mga cloud user sa naaangkop na proyekto. Partikular, inimbitahan at itinalaga nila ang mga user…
examples.suggested: Iniimbitahan ng Cloud administrator… Partikular, iniimbitahan at itinatalaga nila…
examples.why: The surrounding steps use the present/habitual aspect ("Gumagawa ang Cloud administrator"); this step switches to completed past, breaking the instructional voice of the numbered list.

editor_note: One genuine semantic drift: in the Fuchs-van de Graaf figure discussion the English "the upper left-hand corner of the gray region" is rendered "ang kanang sulok sa itaas ng kulay-abong rehiyon" (the upper RIGHT-hand corner) — the direction is inverted, so a learner is pointed at the wrong corner of the plot, contradicting the very sentence that follows it (fidelity near one ⇒ trace distance near zero, which lives at upper-LEFT).
