Secondary: the opening "Given two quantum state vectors" becomes "Dahil sa dalawang quantum state vector" ("Because of two…"), turning a setup clause into a causal one; and the math-variable let-clauses use the personal marker "si" ("Hayaan si $\rho$ at $\sigma$", also at $\mathsf{X}$/$\mathsf{Y}$), which no Tagalog technical writer would use for symbols — "Hayaan ang" is correct. Minor untranslated/odd tokens remain ("ito ay essentially isang bagay ng kagustuhan", "paSimplify natin", "nasisimplify"). Rest of the proof prose (gentle measurement lemma, Uhlmann) is faithful and reads well.

examples.source: The most interesting region of this figure from a practical viewpoint is the upper left-hand corner of the gray region:
examples.translation: Ang pinaka-kawili-wiling rehiyon ng figure na ito mula sa praktikal na pananaw ay ang kanang sulok sa itaas ng kulay-abong rehiyon:
examples.suggested: … ay ang kaliwang sulok sa itaas ng kulay-abong rehiyon:
examples.why: 'kanang' = right; the English says left. The inverted direction sends the reader to the wrong corner of the FvdG plot and contradicts the following clause (fidelity ≈ 1 ⇒ trace distance ≈ 0, i.e. the upper-LEFT corner).

examples.source: Given two quantum state vectors, the fidelity between the pure states associated with these quantum state vectors equals the absolute value of the inner product…
examples.translation: Dahil sa dalawang quantum state vector, ang fidelity sa pagitan ng mga pure state na kaugnay ng mga quantum state vector na ito ay katumbas ng absolute value ng inner product…
examples.suggested: Sa dalawang ibinigay na quantum state vector, ang fidelity …
examples.why: 'Dahil sa' means 'because of / due to', converting the English's hypothesis-setup ('Given …') into a causal claim. Small but it misstates the logical role of the clause that frames the whole section.

examples.source: Let $\rho$ and $\sigma$ be density matrices … / Let $\mathsf{X}$ be a system in a state $\rho$
examples.translation: Hayaan si $\rho$ at $\sigma$ na maging mga density matrix … / Hayaan si $\mathsf{X}$ na maging isang sistema
examples.suggested: Hayaan ang $\rho$ at $\sigma$ na maging … / Hayaan ang $\mathsf{X}$ na …
examples.why: 'si' is the personal-name marker; applying it to mathematical symbols reads as machine translation. A native technical writer uses 'ang'. Recurs consistently in all three let-clauses.

examples.source: it's essentially a matter of preference / let's simplify the expression we're interested in
examples.translation: ito ay essentially isang bagay ng kagustuhan / Una, paSimplify natin ang ekspresyon
examples.suggested: ito ay talagang bagay lamang ng kagustuhan / Una, pasimplehin natin ang ekspresyon
examples.why: 'essentially' is left untranslated mid-sentence, and 'paSimplify' carries stray internal capitalization plus an English stem where 'pasimplehin' (used correctly at line 299) exists — an inconsistency within the same file.

editor_note: Overall faithful and consistent — "ingay" for noise, "sukatan" for metric, "pamamaraan ng kompilasyon" for compilation method, and the kept-English core terms (two-qubit depth, gate count, transpiler, fidelity) hold throughout, and the informal register (maiintindihan mo, natin) is clean with no po/opo. Four genuine meaning slips, none inverting a technical claim: (1) in the mirror-circuit caveat, "can underestimate certain errors" became "maaari nitong maunas ang ilang partikular na error" — "maunas" is not a Tagalog word, so the caveat is unreadable and the reader cannot tell whether errors are under- or over-estimated; (2) in "Mga resulta ng mirror circuit", "This holds on both the noisy simulator ... and real hardware" became "Ito ay nangunguna sa parehong ..." — "nangunguna" means "leads/ranks first", not "remains true", so the sentence asserts a predicate the English does not; (3) "The mirror circuit approach is appealingly simple" became "simpleng kapansin-pansin" ("noticeably simple"), losing the pedagogical point that its simplicity is what makes it attractive; (4) a systematic pattern of dropped intensifiers in the Analysis section — "much higher two-qubit depth"/"correspondingly much lower"/"substantially higher"/"significantly longer" all render as plain "mas mataas"/"mas mababa"/"mas matagal", softening the emphasis that explains why the tfim hardware example is an outlier. Also a stray "sa sa" doubling and the untranslated/misspelled "teoretically" in Step 3.

examples.source: it can underestimate certain errors when noise cancels symmetrically across the mirror boundary
examples.translation: maaari nitong maunas ang ilang partikular na error kapag ang ingay ay nagkakansela nang simetriko sa kabila ng mirror boundary
examples.suggested: maaari nitong maliitin ang ilang partikular na error kapag ang ingay ay nagkakansela nang simetriko sa kabila ng mirror boundary
examples.why: "maunas" is not a Tagalog word; the verb carrying the caveat's whole meaning is unintelligible, so the learner cannot tell in which direction the mirror-circuit estimate is biased.

examples.source: This holds on both the noisy simulator (small-scale) and real hardware (large-scale).
examples.translation: Ito ay nangunguna sa parehong maingay na simulator (maliit na antas) at tunay na hardware (malalaking antas).
examples.suggested: Nananatiling totoo ito sa parehong maingay na simulator (maliit na antas) at tunay na hardware (malalaking antas).
examples.why: "nangunguna" means "leads / ranks first", not "remains true". The sentence should say the depth-vs-fidelity trend is confirmed in both settings; as written it claims something about ranking that the English never asserts.

examples.source: The mirror circuit approach is appealingly simple and scales to any circuit size
examples.translation: Ang mirror circuit approach ay simpleng kapansin-pansin at nag-scale sa anumang laki ng circuit
examples.suggested: Kaakit-akit ang pagiging simple ng mirror circuit approach, at nag-scale ito sa anumang laki ng circuit
examples.why: "simpleng kapansin-pansin" reads as "noticeably simple" and inverts the head/modifier; the English point is that the simplicity is what makes the technique attractive — that motivation is lost.

examples.source: SABRE produces a much higher two-qubit depth than the AI-powered transpiler and Rustiq, so its fidelity is correspondingly much lower
examples.translation: ang SABRE ay gumagawa ng mas mataas na two-qubit depth kaysa sa AI-powered transpiler at Rustiq, kaya ang fidelity nito ay naaayon na mas mababa
examples.suggested: ang SABRE ay gumagawa ng higit na mas mataas na two-qubit depth kaysa sa AI-powered transpiler at Rustiq, kaya ang fidelity nito ay naaayon na higit na mas mababa
examples.why: Both instances of "much" are dropped, softening the claim. The paragraph's whole purpose is to explain that this circuit is an extreme outlier (0.0005 vs 0.3267 fidelity); without the intensifiers the reader loses why the result is unrepresentative. The same flattening recurs with "substantially higher" (line ~1360) and "significantly longer" (line ~1366).

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — no inverted conditions, dropped caveats, or hallucinated claims; the tricky V1→V2 primitives passage ("Hindi pinahintulutan ng V1 interface ang parehong flexibility... ang bilang ng mga input na circuit ay kailangang tumugma sa bilang ng mga observable at parameter set") and the ScalableSymbolicPulse "Hindi na kailangan ng anumang migration action" caveat both land correctly, and terminology (qubit, gate, circuit, observable, expectation value → "expectation value", shots, transpile, backend, Sampler/Estimator, pub/PUB) is kept in English consistently, matching the field convention. Two real lexical/grammar defects: in the pulse `library` section the verb "nagge-geser" is not a Tagalog word at all (it is Indonesian/Malay "geser" = to shift, apparently contaminated in) where the source says a phase of 2π "shifts by a full cycle" — should be "nagsi-shift"/"naglilipat"; and in the basicaer "Mga global na instance" caution, "Wala nang kapalit para sa ang `BasicAer` global instance" is ungrammatical ("para sa ang" — the marker `ang` must be dropped). Neither misleads a learner, so this is MINOR, not FAIL. A third smaller slip: "To ease the migration process" is rendered "Para mapabilis ang proseso ng migration" (= to speed up), a slight sense shift from "ease".

examples.source: the phase of both Sawtooth and Square is defined such that a phase of `2\pi` shifts by a full cycle, contrary to the discrete counterpart
examples.translation: ang phase ng parehong Sawtooth at Square ay tinukoy nang ang phase na `2\pi` ay nagge-geser ng isang buong cycle, salungat sa discrete na katumbas
examples.suggested: ... ay tinukoy nang ang phase na `2\pi` ay nagsi-shift ng isang buong cycle, salungat sa discrete na katumbas
examples.why: "nagge-geser" is not Tagalog — "geser" is the Indonesian/Malay verb for "to shift", contaminated into the text. A Tagalog reader cannot parse it; the field convention here would be "nag-shift" or "naglilipat".

examples.source: There is no replacement for the `BasicAer` global instance that could be directly imported as `qiskit.BasicAer`.
examples.translation: Wala nang kapalit para sa ang `BasicAer` global instance na maaaring direktang i-import bilang `qiskit.BasicAer`.
examples.suggested: Wala nang kapalit para sa `BasicAer` global instance na maaaring direktang i-import bilang `qiskit.BasicAer`.
examples.why: "para sa ang" is ungrammatical — the preposition "para sa" cannot be followed by the marker "ang". Reads as an unedited MT artifact, though the meaning survives.

examples.source: To ease the migration process, you can use the `flake8-qiskit-migration` tool
examples.translation: Para mapabilis ang proseso ng migration, maaari kang gumamit ng `flake8-qiskit-migration` tool
examples.suggested: Para mapadali ang proseso ng migration, maaari kang gumamit ng `flake8-qiskit-migration` tool
examples.why: "mapabilis" means "to speed up", not "to ease/make easier". A mild sense shift — the tool's value is that it finds removed imports for you, not that it makes migration faster.

editor_note: One outright meaning-breaking error: in the **Mga institusyong akademiko** paragraph, "training the next generation of quantum scientists and engineers" is rendered "habang sinasakal ang susunod na henerasyon ng mga quantum scientist at engineer" — "sinasakal" means "strangling/choking", the near-opposite of the source's claim about academia nurturing new talent (correct verb: "sinasanay"). This is a fluent-sounding sentence asserting something the English does not, so it misleads a learner and forces a FAIL on its own. Secondary, non-misleading slips: "fuels innovation" → "nagpapalakad ng innovation" (odd; "nagpapasigla/nagtutulak" is the natural rendering), the calque "ilagay sa lugar" for "have in place" in the contents list, and "nagbibigay ng mga mag-aaral" (should be "nagbibigay sa mga mag-aaral") in the community paragraph. Register is correctly informal throughout — no po/opo — and the domain nouns (quantum computing, ecosystem, QIC, grant writing) are consistently kept in English as the field does.

examples.source: they play a central role by pushing the boundaries of theory and experimentation while training the next generation of quantum scientists and engineers
examples.translation: gumaganap sila ng sentral na papel sa pamamagitan ng pagtutulak sa mga hangganan ng teorya at eksperimento habang sinasakal ang susunod na henerasyon ng mga quantum scientist at engineer
examples.suggested: ... habang sinasanay ang susunod na henerasyon ng mga quantum scientist at engineer
examples.why: 'sinasakal' = strangling/choking. The source says academic institutions TRAIN the next generation; the Tagalog says they strangle them. Fluent, grammatical, and flatly contrary to the source — the single clearest hallucination in the file.

examples.source: providing deep domain expertise that fuels innovation across industry and academia
examples.translation: pagbibigay ng malalim na domain expertise na nagpapalakad ng innovation sa industriya at akademya
examples.suggested: ... na nagpapasigla/nagtutulak ng innovation sa industriya at akademya
examples.why: 'nagpapalakad' literally means 'makes walk / operates'; a native technical writer would use 'nagpapasigla' or 'nagtutulak' for 'fuels'. Meaning survives, but the metaphor is broken.

examples.source: things to consider and have in place prior to large purchases of quantum computing time
examples.translation: mga bagay na dapat isaalang-alang at ilagay sa lugar bago mag-purchase ng malaking oras ng quantum computing
examples.suggested: mga bagay na dapat isaalang-alang at maihanda bago bumili ng malaking bahagi ng oras ng quantum computing
examples.why: 'ilagay sa lugar' is a word-for-word calque of 'have in place' and reads as machine output; 'maihanda' is the idiomatic Tagalog.

examples.source: equip learners with hands-on skills early
examples.translation: nagbibigay ng mga mag-aaral ng mga hands-on na kasanayan nang maaga
examples.suggested: nagbibigay sa mga mag-aaral ng mga hands-on na kasanayan nang maaga
examples.why: Wrong case marker on the recipient ('ng' instead of 'sa'), which momentarily reads as 'giving away the students'. Isolated grammar slip, not misleading in context.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — the tricky Background passage (mixed state at the cut, tomographically complete Pauli basis, "sukatin nang maraming beses sa iba't ibang bases ... at naaangkop na inihanda sa kanyang eigenstate") and the noiseless-simulator caveat ("ang expectation value na nakuha na may at walang circuit cutting ay magiging pareho") both land correctly, and domain terms (qubit, gate, observable, expectation value, subcircuit, transpile, backend) are kept in English consistently. Three isolated slips, none misleading: (1) "may parameter na dalawang parameters" in the MBL intro is a redundant double-rendering of "is parameterized by two parameters"; (2) "hatiin ang circuit kasabay ng `Move` gate" renders "partitioned along the `Move` gate" as "together with / at the same time as", where the earlier "sa kahabaan ng wire" convention for "along" was correct; (3) "Tulad ng tinatalakay dati" should be past "tinalakay" ("As discussed before"), and "tiyaking mayroon kayong" in Requirements is a mild plural/formal drift from the file's otherwise casual register. No drift, hallucination, or dropped caveat found in the last 40% — the large-scale hardware section and Next steps track the English exactly.

examples.source: The MBL circuit is a hardware-efficient circuit and is parameterized by two parameters $\theta$ and $\vec{\phi}$.
examples.translation: Ang MBL circuit ay isang hardware-efficient circuit at may parameter na dalawang parameters na $\theta$ at $\vec{\phi}$.
examples.suggested: Ang MBL circuit ay isang hardware-efficient circuit na may dalawang parameter, $\theta$ at $\vec{\phi}$.
examples.why: "may parameter na dalawang parameters" duplicates the noun and reads as raw MT; a native technical writer would just say "may dalawang parameter".

examples.source: Now the circuit can be partitioned along the `Move` gate and we obtain the subcircuits...
examples.translation: Ngayon ay maaari nang hatiin ang circuit kasabay ng `Move` gate at makukuha natin ang mga subcircuits...
examples.suggested: Ngayon ay maaari nang hatiin ang circuit sa kahabaan ng `Move` gate ...
examples.why: "kasabay ng" means "simultaneously with", not "along"; the file already uses the correct "sa kahabaan ng" for "along the wire" in Background, so this is also an internal inconsistency.

examples.source: As discussed before, for each cut the upstream circuit must be measured in a Pauli basis...
examples.translation: Tulad ng tinatalakay dati, para sa bawat pagputol ang upstream circuit ay kailangang sukatin sa isang Pauli basis...
examples.suggested: Tulad ng tinalakay dati, ...
examples.why: Aspect error: "tinatalakay" is progressive ("as is being discussed"); the English back-reference needs the completed form "tinalakay".

editor_note: The verb "solve" is mistranslated as "magsulat" ("to write") in two separate places, both of which mislead: line ~105 renders "you can also solve weighted graph problems" as "maaari ka ring magsulat ng mga weighted graph problem", and line ~199 renders "You can solve arbitrary problem types by inputting the problem definition..." as "Maaari kang magsulat ng arbitrary na uri ng problema...". This tells the learner the Solver lets them *author* problems rather than *solve* them — the exact capability claim of the page — and it is doubly odd since the same file correctly uses "lutasin/malutas" everywhere else (e.g. "Para malutas ang isang pangkalahatang problema"). Two additional focus/voice errors garble the actor-object relation: "maaari kang malutas ng utility-scale na mga optimization problem" (should be "malutas mo" / "lumutas ka ng") and "Parehong malulutas ng unconstrained at constrained na mga problema", which literally reads "solvable BY unconstrained and constrained problems" instead of "Malulutas ang parehong unconstrained at constrained na problema". Terminology otherwise fine and consistent (qubit, graph, penalty term, cost function, vertex cover all kept in English per convention) and the informal register holds with no po/opo.

examples.source: but you can also solve weighted graph problems.
examples.translation: ngunit maaari ka ring magsulat ng mga weighted graph problem.
examples.suggested: ngunit maaari ka ring lumutas ng mga weighted graph problem.
examples.why: "magsulat" = "to write". The source says the Solver can also SOLVE weighted graphs; the translation asserts the reader can write them, changing the stated capability.

examples.source: You can solve arbitrary problem types by inputting the problem definition represented as a polynomial where constraints are modeled as penalty terms.
examples.translation: Maaari kang magsulat ng arbitrary na uri ng problema sa pamamagitan ng pag-input ng depinisyon ng problema bilang polynomial kung saan ang mga constraint ay kinakatawan bilang penalty term.
examples.suggested: Maaari kang lumutas ng arbitrary na uri ng problema sa pamamagitan ng pag-input ng depinisyon ng problema...
examples.why: Same solve→write substitution. Nonsensical in context: you do not "write" a problem type by inputting its definition; the Solver solves it.

examples.source: Both unconstrained and constrained problems are solvable, given that constraints can be formulated as penalty terms.
examples.translation: Parehong malulutas ng unconstrained at constrained na mga problema, basta ma-formulate ang mga constraint bilang penalty term.
examples.suggested: Malulutas ang parehong unconstrained at constrained na mga problema, basta ma-formulate ang mga constraint bilang penalty term.
examples.why: The marker "ng" makes the problems the ACTOR ("solvable by ... problems"); the object marker "ang" is required. A native technical writer would not produce this focus error.

examples.source: you can solve utility-scale optimization problems on quantum hardware
examples.translation: maaari kang malutas ng utility-scale na mga optimization problem sa quantum hardware
examples.suggested: malulutas mo ang mga utility-scale na optimization problem sa quantum hardware
examples.why: "maaari kang malutas ng" mixes actor-focus pronoun with an object-focus verb; reads as MT, not native Tagalog.

editor_note: Faithful, natural informal Tagalog throughout: course title kept in English with a proper "Ang ... ay isang libreng online na kurso" frame, domain terms correctly kept (cryptographic hash functions, symmetric/asymmetric key cryptography, quantum-safe cryptography), and the full Credly privacy notice ("PAUNAWA: Ginagamit ng IBM ang mga serbisyo ng Credly ... third-party na data processor ... matatagpuan sa Estados Unidos") renders every clause of the English with no dropped caveat; only nit is the mildly modal "maaari ring tuklasin" for "as well as explore", which does not mislead.

Meaning is faithful throughout — all 14 numbered steps, the scoping caveat in step 11, and the closing "Creating custom roles" pointer track the English, and the informal "ти" convention holds consistently (натисни, введи, перегляни, "ти створив(ла)"). Three non-misleading slips: (1) "dropdown menu" is rendered "випадне меню" twice (steps 1 and 6) — the standard Ukrainian UI term is "спадне меню" (or "випадне спискове меню"), and "випадне" reads as a calque-adjacent coinage; (2) step 14's closing line has a typo, "перегляди теми" instead of "переглянь теми"; (3) step 11's caveat "якщо не обмежити доступ усіма ресурсами" inverts the natural framing of "scope the access to all resources" — "обмежити … усіма ресурсами" (restrict with all resources) is self-contradictory phrasing, though the intent survives from context. Nothing here would mislead a learner into a wrong action.

examples.source: Select the Qiskit Runtime service from the dropdown menu … In the Service dropdown menu, choose Qiskit Runtime.
examples.translation: Вибери сервіс Qiskit Runtime із випадного меню … У випадному меню Service вибери Qiskit Runtime.
examples.suggested: із спадного меню … У спадному меню Service
examples.why: "Спадне меню" is the conventional Ukrainian UI rendering of "dropdown"; "випадне меню" is a non-standard coinage that a native technical writer would not use. Consistent within the file, so cosmetic rather than confusing.

examples.source: To learn more, see the [Creating custom roles] and the [What are IAM policies…] topics.
examples.translation: Щоб дізнатися більше, перегляди теми [Creating custom roles] і [What are IAM policies and who can assign them?].
examples.suggested: Щоб дізнатися більше, переглянь теми …
examples.why: "Перегляди" is not a valid imperative — a typo for "переглянь". Breaks the otherwise consistent informal imperative register in the file's final sentence.

examples.source: (Note that if you do not scope the access to all resources, the Analytics Viewer role will not be able to see analytics from all instances.)
examples.translation: (Зверни увагу: якщо не обмежити доступ усіма ресурсами, роль Analytics Viewer не зможе переглядати аналітику з усіх екземплярів.)
examples.suggested: (Зверни увагу: якщо не задати область доступу як «All resources», роль Analytics Viewer не зможе переглядати аналітику з усіх екземплярів.)
examples.why: "Обмежити доступ усіма ресурсами" reads as "restrict access with all resources", which fights the intended sense of setting the scope to the All resources option. The caveat is not lost — the step just above names "All resources" explicitly — but the wording flattens the instruction's clarity.

editor_note: One genuine negation inversion and one recurring wrong term. (1) The English "vectors representing quantum states *need not* have nonnegative real number entries" is rendered "не повинні мати невід'ємні дійсні числа як елементи" — in Ukrainian "не повинні" reads as a prohibition ("must not have"), turning a permissive statement into a false rule that a learner will read as forbidding |0⟩ = (1,0)ᵀ, whose entries ARE nonnegative reals. It should be "не обов'язково мають". (2) "valid quantum state vectors" is rendered "дійсні вектори квантових станів" twice (lines ~83, ~108); in a linear-algebra context "дійсний" means real-valued, and line 83 immediately says "їхні елементи є комплексними числами" — the sentence contradicts itself. Correct: "допустимими"/"коректними". Remaining slips are naturalness only: "деякі часто зустрічаються унітарні операції" / "кілька часто зустрічаються векторів станів" uses a finite verb where a participle is needed ("поширені"/"часто вживані"), and "ми будемо стосуватися систем" is a calque of "we'll be concerned with" (should be "матимемо справу з системами"). Register is correctly informal "ти" throughout (Зверни увагу, Уяви, май на увазі); core terms (кубіт, суперпозиція, унітарна матриця, вимірювання в стандартному базисі, правило Борна, нотація Дірака, квантове перетворення Фур'є) are correct and consistent.

examples.source: Thus, in contrast to probabilistic states, vectors representing quantum states need not have nonnegative real number entries
examples.translation: Таким чином, на відміну від ймовірнісних станів, вектори, що представляють квантові стани, не повинні мати невід'ємні дійсні числа як елементи
examples.suggested: …не обов'язково мають невід'ємні дійсні числа як елементи
examples.why: Negation inversion: 'не повинні' = 'must not', turning permission ('need not') into prohibition. A learner would conclude nonnegative real entries are disallowed, contradicting the |0⟩ and |1⟩ examples given two paragraphs later.

examples.source: standard basis elements are valid quantum state vectors: their entries are complex numbers
examples.translation: елементи стандартного базису є дійсними векторами квантових станів: їхні елементи є комплексними числами
examples.suggested: …є допустимими векторами квантових станів…
examples.why: 'дійсний' in a linear-algebra text reads as 'real-valued', not 'valid' — the sentence then contradicts its own clause about complex entries. Same error repeats at ~line 108 ('Тому це дійсні вектори квантових станів').

examples.source: The following list describes some commonly encountered unitary operations on qubits.
examples.translation: Наступний список описує деякі часто зустрічаються унітарні операції на кубітах.
examples.suggested: …деякі поширені унітарні операції на кубітах.
examples.why: 'часто зустрічаються' is a finite verb used as an attributive modifier — ungrammatical; a native technical writer would use 'поширені' or 'часто вживані'. Recurs at ~line 499 ('кілька часто зустрічаються векторів станів кубіта').

examples.source: we'll be concerned with systems having finite and nonempty sets of classical states
examples.translation: ми будемо стосуватися систем, що мають скінченні та непорожні множини класичних станів
examples.suggested: ми матимемо справу з системами, що мають скінченні та непорожні множини класичних станів
examples.why: Calque of 'be concerned with'; 'ми будемо стосуватися' is not idiomatic Ukrainian — the subject cannot 'concern' the systems in this construction.

editor_note: The physicist's name "Trotter" is rendered as the non-word "Трожого" in all three places it occurs ("розкладом Трожого (Lie Trotter)", "чотирьох кроків Трожого", "похибки Трожого") — it is not a Ukrainian form of Троттер and a learner cannot recover the intended term; the correct rendering is "розклад Лі–Троттера" / "кроки Троттера" / "похибка Троттера". Separately there is a real semantic drift in the slicing section: EN "we will group gates of the same type into slices" became "ми згрупуємо вентилі за глибиною у зрізи" (grouped *by depth*), which states a different grouping criterion than the source and contradicts the tutorial's own explanation that slice choice is what matters. Everything else is meaning-faithful — the OBP background (absorbing $U_C$ into the observable, truncation error budget, "перевищить вісім" for "grows past eight", the noiseless-simulator caveat) is accurate, and the informal "ти" register holds throughout ("Зверни увагу", "переконайся, що встановив").

examples.source: with Lie Trotter decomposition / four Trotter steps / Multi-product formulas to reduce Trotter error
examples.translation: з розкладом Трожого (Lie Trotter) / чотирьох кроків Трожого / Формули з кількома добутками для зменшення похибки Трожого
examples.suggested: з розкладом Лі–Троттера / чотирьох кроків Троттера / … для зменшення похибки Троттера
examples.why: "Трожого" is not a Ukrainian word or a declension of Троттер; the eponym is unrecoverable for the reader, and it recurs three times so it is systematic, not a typo.

examples.source: Here, we will group gates of the same type into slices using the slice_by_depth function.
examples.translation: Тут ми згрупуємо вентилі за глибиною у зрізи за допомогою функції slice_by_depth.
examples.suggested: Тут ми згрупуємо вентилі одного типу у зрізи за допомогою функції slice_by_depth.
examples.why: "of the same type" was replaced by "за глибиною" (by depth), asserting a different slicing criterion than the source states.

examples.source: The backpropagated circuit and the backpropagated operator give the exact outcome as the original circuit and operator.
examples.translation: Ланцюг зі зворотним поширенням та оператор зі зворотним поширенням дають точний результат, як початковий ланцюг та оператор.
examples.suggested: … дають точно такий самий результат, як початковий ланцюг та оператор.
examples.why: "дають точний результат, як…" is a calque; the comparative "такий самий" is missing, so the sentence reads broken rather than expressing exact equality.

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — the CRN caveat block is rendered correctly and completely (optional CRN → "Якщо CRN передано, доступні лише бекенди та завдання з цього екземпляра"; "If an instance is not specified" → "Якщо екземпляр не вказано, доступні всі бекенди..."), the deprecated-provider caveat keeps both the "also deprecated" parenthetical and the "більше недоступні" claim, all table numbers match, and the informal "ти" convention holds ("дивись", "Використовуй", "твоєму обліковому записі"). Two non-misleading slips: (1) the Osprey bullet ends in an ungrammatical phrase — "в межах тієї самої трасування проводки" — feminine "тієї самої" agrees with nothing ("трасування" is neuter), so the rendering of "within the same wiring footprint" is broken Ukrainian a native writer would not produce; (2) "packaging" is split across the two adjacent bullets — Osprey uses "корпусуванні пристрою" while Eagle uses "технології пакування" for the same concept in consecutive lines. Neither changes the meaning; core terms (кубіт, двокубітні вентилі, бекенди, перестроювані зв'язувачі) are correct and consistent.

examples.source: as well as custom flex cabling in the cryostat to fit the greater I/O requirements within the same wiring footprint.
examples.translation: а також спеціальних гнучких кабелів у кріостаті для задоволення більших вимог введення/виведення в межах тієї самої трасування проводки.
examples.suggested: а також спеціальних гнучких кабелів у кріостаті, щоб задовольнити зрослі вимоги до введення/виведення в межах того самого обсягу проводки.
examples.why: "тієї самої трасування" is ungrammatical — the feminine demonstrative does not agree with the neuter noun "трасування"; the phrase reads as unedited MT rather than a native rendering of "wiring footprint".

examples.source: further enhancements to device packaging … more scalable packaging technologies
examples.translation: додаткових удосконалень у корпусуванні пристрою … більш масштабовані технології пакування
examples.suggested: use one rendering for both, e.g. "корпусування" in both bullets
examples.why: The same English term "packaging" is rendered two different ways in consecutive bullets (Osprey vs Eagle), which reads as inconsistent within a single short list.

editor_note: The prose is generally fluent, informal-"ти" throughout, and core terms are consistent (кубіт, вентиль, схема, гамільтоніан, спостережувана, бекенд, транспілятор, примітиви), but the opening of Experiment 2 mistranslates the central pedagogical point: EN "now that we've seen how to sample a quantum state" becomes "як вибирати квантовий стан" ("how to CHOOSE a quantum state"), which is exactly what the Map step did and not what `Sampler` does — a learner is told the wrong thing about the Sampler/Estimator contrast the whole lesson is built on. Secondary slips: `counts` rendered as "лічильників" (counters) instead of відліків/результатів вимірювань, so "більшість лічильників припадає на 01 або 10" reads as counters rather than measurement outcomes; "given the quantum state" calqued as the ungrammatical "ми хочемо знати, задано квантовий стан, яке значення фізичної величини"; and "антиферромагнітну" carries a Russian-style double -рр- (UA: антиферомагнітну). Also, EN "with the addition of making sure our observables are also written in a way the computer can understand" is softened to "з тією різницею, що наші спостережувані також записуються…", dropping the "we must ensure" obligation.

examples.source: Now that we've seen how to sample a quantum state, let's use Estimator to compute the energy of our Bell state
examples.translation: Тепер, коли ми побачили, як вибирати квантовий стан, давай використаємо Estimator для обчислення енергії нашого стану Белла
examples.suggested: Тепер, коли ми побачили, як робити вибірку з квантового стану (семплювати квантовий стан), давай використаємо Estimator…
examples.why: 'вибирати' = 'to choose/select', not 'to sample'. It reassigns Experiment 1's lesson from measuring the outcome distribution to picking a state — the exact Sampler-vs-Estimator distinction the lesson teaches.

examples.source: We see that the majority of counts are in either `01` or `10`
examples.translation: Ми бачимо, що більшість лічильників припадає або на `01`, або на `10`
examples.suggested: Ми бачимо, що більшість відліків (результатів вимірювань) припадає або на `01`, або на `10`
examples.why: 'лічильник' means a counter device; the measurement-statistics sense of 'counts' is відліки/кількості результатів. As written the sentence describes counters landing on bitstrings.

examples.source: we want to know, given the quantum state, what is the value of a physical quantity, like energy?
examples.translation: ми хочемо знати, задано квантовий стан, яке значення фізичної величини, наприклад енергії?
examples.suggested: ми хочемо знати, яке значення фізичної величини, наприклад енергії, має система за заданого квантового стану
examples.why: 'задано квантовий стан' is a word-for-word calque of the English absolute construction and is ungrammatical as a parenthetical in Ukrainian; no native technical writer would produce it.

examples.source: The optimization step proceeds as before, with the addition of making sure our observables are also written in a way that the quantum computer can understand.
examples.translation: Крок оптимізації відбувається, як і раніше, з тією різницею, що наші спостережувані також записуються у спосіб, який квантовий комп'ютер може зрозуміти.
examples.suggested: …з тим доповненням, що ми маємо подбати, щоб наші спостережувані також були записані у спосіб, зрозумілий квантовому комп'ютеру.
examples.why: 'making sure' (an action the reader must take, implemented by apply_layout) is flattened into a passive statement that it simply happens; the instructional obligation is lost.

editor_note: Faithful and natural throughout with consistent informal "ти"; the tricky bits land correctly — "job.result() є блокувальним викликом до завершення завдання", "включно із завданнями від виведених з експлуатації QPU (квантових процесорів)", and the caveat "сторінка Analytics доступна лише для облікових записів, якими ти володієш або керуєш" all preserve the English condition and scope; UI labels (Workloads/Status/Actions/Cancel) correctly kept in English, "проміжки виконання Sampler" is a defensible rendering of execution spans.

Meaning-faithful and natively phrased throughout; OR branch conditions, the phase-kickback workspace-qubit caveat ("залишається в стані |−⟩ ... або просто відкинутий"), the query-cost asymmetry ("$Z_f$ потребує одного запиту, тоді як $Z_{\mathrm{OR}}$ — жодного") and the final "(якщо він існує) з великою ймовірністю" caveat all track the English, with consistent terminology (query-гейт, phase kickback, OR→АБО, кубіт) and unbroken informal ти (Ініціалізуй/Застосуй/Виміряй/Зверни увагу).

The Overview contains a genuine directional inversion: "you can easily use it in place of the standard Qiskit Runtime primitives" became "її легко замінити стандартними [примітивами Qiskit Runtime]" — in Ukrainian «замінити X Y» (acc.+instr.) means "replace X with Y", so the sentence tells the learner the Q-CTRL function can be swapped out FOR the standard primitives, the exact opposite of the page's selling point (and contradicts the correctly-rendered parallel sentence in "Опис": «ти можеш легко замінити їх Q-CTRL Sampler і Estimator»). Separately, "observable" is rendered throughout as «спостережна» ("observational"), not the conventional Ukrainian domain term «спостережувана (величина)» / «обсервабль» — consistent, but the wrong word form. Minor drift also in the Benchmarks table intro, where "rough thresholds where you can expect extremely consistent solution accuracy" is strengthened to «стабільно високої точності рішення» (adds "high", which the English does not claim). Otherwise the prose is fluent, informal-ти register holds, and core terms (кубіт, гейт, схема, бекенд, математичне сподівання, вимірювання (shots)) are correct and consistent.

examples.source: The Performance Management function can be used with any algorithm, and you can easily use it in place of the standard Qiskit Runtime primitives.
examples.translation: Функцію Performance Management можна використовувати з будь-яким алгоритмом, і її легко замінити стандартними [примітивами Qiskit Runtime].
examples.suggested: ...і нею легко замінити стандартні [примітиви Qiskit Runtime] / ...і її легко використовувати замість стандартних [примітивів Qiskit Runtime].
examples.why: «замінити її стандартними примітивами» = replace the function WITH the standard primitives — the direction is inverted; the English says use the function INSTEAD OF the standard primitives.

examples.source: determine the expectation value of a single circuit-observable pair / generate a random Hermitian operator and an observable
examples.translation: значення математичного сподівання для пари схема–спостережна / згенеруй випадковий ермітовий оператор і спостережну
examples.suggested: пари схема–спостережувана / ермітовий оператор і спостережувану (величину)
examples.why: «спостережна» means 'observational'; the established Ukrainian physics term for an observable is «спостережувана (величина)» (or «обсервабль»).

examples.source: represents rough thresholds where you can expect extremely consistent solution accuracy
examples.translation: приблизні пороги, за яких можна очікувати стабільно високої точності рішення
examples.suggested: приблизні пороги, за яких можна очікувати надзвичайно стабільної точності розв'язку
examples.why: Adds a claim of HIGH accuracy that the English does not make — it claims only consistency, and drops the intensifier 'extremely'.
